```r
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Read spec files
by_vars   <- read_tsv("inputs/by_vars.tsv",   show_col_types = FALSE) %>% pull(1)
id_vars   <- read_tsv("inputs/id_vars.tsv",   show_col_types = FALSE) %>% pull(1)
key_var   <- read_tsv("inputs/key_var.tsv",   show_col_types = FALSE) %>% pull(1)
relationship <- read_tsv("inputs/relationship.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>% pull(1)
value_var <- read_tsv("inputs/value_var.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>% pull(1)

# Read filter expression
filter_expr <- read_tsv("inputs/filter.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  pull(1) %>%
  .[. != "filter"]
filter_expr <- filter_expr[1]

# Read datasets
dataset_main  <- read_tsv("inputs/datase.tsv",        show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset_merge <- read_tsv("inputs/dataset_merge.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Apply filter to both datasets (on main structure)
if (!is.na(filter_expr) && nzchar(filter_expr)) {
  dataset_main  <- dataset_main  %>% filter(!!rlang::parse_expr(filter_expr))
  dataset_merge <- dataset_merge %>% filter(!!rlang::parse_expr(filter_expr))
}

# Ensure columns are present
all_needed <- unique(c(by_vars, id_vars, key_var, relationship, value_var))
missing_main  <- setdiff(all_needed, names(dataset_main))
missing_merge <- setdiff(all_needed, names(dataset_merge))
if (length(missing_main) > 0) {
  stop("Missing columns in datase.tsv: ", paste(missing_main, collapse = ", "))
}
if (length(missing_merge) > 0) {
  stop("Missing columns in dataset_merge.tsv: ", paste(missing_merge, collapse = ", "))
}

# For derive_vars_transposed-style operation:
# - BY variables define grouping.
# - value_var define variables to transpose.
# - key_var defines the column that will become part of the new column names.
# - relationship can be used as additional part for column naming (here concatenated).
# We will:
#   1. Restrict both datasets to BY + key_var + relationship + value_var.
#   2. Add a source identifier (e.g., SRC="A" for main, "B" for merge).
#   3. Bind rows.
#   4. Pivot wider: new columns from key_var + SRC, values from value_var (one row per BY).

# Prepare datasets in long format
prep_long <- function(df, src_flag) {
  df %>%
    select(all_of(unique(c(by_vars, key_var, relationship, value_var)))) %>%
    mutate(.SRC = src_flag) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = all_of(value_var),
      names_to   = ".VALNAME",
      values_to  = ".VAL"
    )
}

long_main  <- prep_long(dataset_main,  "A")
long_merge <- prep_long(dataset_merge, "B")

long_all <- bind_rows(long_main, long_merge)

# Build column key from key_var, relationship, .SRC, and value var name
# Example: AVAL for PARAMCD=SYSBP, SRC=A -> SYSBP_A_AVAL
long_all <- long_all %>%
  unite(
    ".KEY",
    c(all_of(key_var), ".SRC", ".VALNAME"),
    sep = "_",
    remove = FALSE
  )

# Now pivot wider so that we have one row per BY vars and relationship vars
# and columns for each key combination
result <- long_all %>%
  select(all_of(by_vars), all_of(relationship), .KEY, .VAL) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = .KEY,
    values_from = .VAL
  ) %>%
  arrange(across(all_of(by_vars)))

# Write to CSV
write_csv(result, "outputs/result.csv", na = "")
```

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Read spec files
by_vars   <- read_tsv("inputs/by_vars.tsv",   show_col_types = FALSE) %>% pull(1)
id_vars   <- read_tsv("inputs/id_vars.tsv",   show_col_types = FALSE) %>% pull(1)
key_var   <- read_tsv("inputs/key_var.tsv",   show_col_types = FALSE) %>% pull(1)
relationship <- read_tsv("inputs/relationship.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>% pull(1)
value_var <- read_tsv("inputs/value_var.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>% pull(1)

# Read filter expression
filter_expr <- read_tsv("inputs/filter.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  pull(1) %>%
  .[. != "filter"]
filter_expr <- filter_expr[1]

# Read datasets
dataset_main  <- read_tsv("inputs/datase.tsv",        show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset_merge <- read_tsv("inputs/dataset_merge.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Apply filter to both datasets (on main structure)
if (!is.na(filter_expr) && nzchar(filter_expr)) {
  dataset_main  <- dataset_main  %>% filter(!!rlang::parse_expr(filter_expr))
  dataset_merge <- dataset_merge %>% filter(!!rlang::parse_expr(filter_expr))
}

# Ensure columns are present
all_needed <- unique(c(by_vars, id_vars, key_var, relationship, value_var))
missing_main  <- setdiff(all_needed, names(dataset_main))
missing_merge <- setdiff(all_needed, names(dataset_merge))
if (length(missing_main) > 0) {
  stop("Missing columns in datase.tsv: ", paste(missing_main, collapse = ", "))
}
if (length(missing_merge) > 0) {
  stop("Missing columns in dataset_merge.tsv: ", paste(missing_merge, collapse = ", "))
}

# For derive_vars_transposed-style operation:
# - BY variables define grouping.
# - value_var define variables to transpose.
# - key_var defines the column that will become part of the new column names.
# - relationship can be used as additional part for column naming (here concatenated).
# We will:
#   1. Restrict both datasets to BY + key_var + relationship + value_var.
#   2. Add a source identifier (e.g., SRC="A" for main, "B" for merge).
#   3. Bind rows.
#   4. Pivot wider: new columns from key_var + SRC, values from value_var (one row per BY).

# Prepare datasets in long format
prep_long <- function(df, src_flag) {
  df %>%
    select(all_of(unique(c(by_vars, key_var, relationship, value_var)))) %>%
    mutate(.SRC = src_flag) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = all_of(value_var),
      names_to   = ".VALNAME",
      values_to  = ".VAL"
    )
}

long_main  <- prep_long(dataset_main,  "A")
long_merge <- prep_long(dataset_merge, "B")

long_all <- bind_rows(long_main, long_merge)

# Build column key from key_var, relationship, .SRC, and value var name
# Example: AVAL for PARAMCD=SYSBP, SRC=A -> SYSBP_A_AVAL
long_all <- long_all %>%
  unite(
    ".KEY",
    c(all_of(key_var), ".SRC", ".VALNAME"),
    sep = "_",
    remove = FALSE
  )

# Now pivot wider so that we have one row per BY vars and relationship vars
# and columns for each key combination
result <- long_all %>%
  select(all_of(by_vars), all_of(relationship), .KEY, .VAL) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = .KEY,
    values_from = .VAL
  ) %>%
  arrange(across(all_of(by_vars)))

# Write to CSV
write_csv(result, "outputs/result.csv", na = "")

## Inputs preview (no reference answers)

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(admiral)

# Helper to read single-cell TSVs (header + 1 value)
read_single_value <- function(path) {
  x <- read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  x[[1]][1]
}

# Inputs directory
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read control TSVs
dataset_name <- read_single_value(file.path(in_dir, "dataset_name.tsv"))
date_imp     <- read_single_value(file.path(in_dir, "date.tsv"))
filter_expr  <- read_single_value(file.path(in_dir, "filter.tsv"))
mode_main    <- read_single_value(file.path(in_dir, "mode.tsv"))
mode_df      <- read_single_value(file.path(in_dir, "mode_df.tsv"))
order_main   <- read_single_value(file.path(in_dir, "order.tsv"))
order_df     <- read_single_value(file.path(in_dir, "order_df.tsv"))
set_values   <- read_single_value(file.path(in_dir, "set_values_to.tsv"))

# Read date metadata: list of variable names
date_df <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "date_df.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
date_vars <- setdiff(names(date_df), "id")

# Read dthcaus & dthcaus_df: first column named "dt"
dthcaus    <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "dthcaus.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
dthcaus_df <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "dthcaus_df.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# Read main dataset (dataset_name.tsv says "adsl")
main_path <- file.path(in_dir, paste0(dataset_name, ".tsv"))
adsl <- read_tsv(main_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Prepare order vectors
order_main_vars <- str_split(order_main, ",")[[1]] |> str_trim()
order_df_vars   <- str_split(order_df, ",")[[1]]   |> str_trim()

# Convert dt to Date in both dthcaus tables
dthcaus    <- dthcaus    %>% mutate(dt = as.Date(dt))
dthcaus_df <- dthcaus_df %>% mutate(dt = as.Date(dt))

# Attach row index to dthcaus_df for reproducible selection
dthcaus_df <- dthcaus_df %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())

# Sort dthcaus_df according to order_df (if present in data)
for (v in order_df_vars) {
  if (v %in% names(dthcaus_df)) {
    dthcaus_df <- dthcaus_df %>% arrange(.data[[v]], .by_group = FALSE)
  }
}

# Select first/last per dt from dthcaus_df according to mode_df
if (mode_df == "first") {
  dthcaus_df_sel <- dthcaus_df %>%
    group_by(dt) %>%
    slice(1L) %>%
    ungroup()
} else if (mode_df == "last") {
  dthcaus_df_sel <- dthcaus_df %>%
    group_by(dt) %>%
    slice(n()) %>%
    ungroup()
} else {
  dthcaus_df_sel <- dthcaus_df
}

# Join dthcaus (unique dt) to selected dthcaus_df on dt
# If multiple rows in dthcaus_df_sel per dt, keep all matches
dth_join <- dthcaus %>%
  left_join(dthcaus_df_sel, by = "dt", suffix = c("", "_df"))

# Apply date imputation to dt (if partial dates existed; here assume complete)
# Using admiral::convert_blanks_to_na + derive_vars_dt if needed;
# in this example dt already Date, so just keep as is

# If AVAL present, filter and derive using set_values_to
if ("AVAL" %in% names(dth_join)) {
  dth_join <- dth_join %>%
    filter(eval(parse(text = filter_expr)))
  # Apply set_values_to expression within mutate
  # set_values is like 'exprs(AVAL = mean(AVAL, na.rm=TRUE))'
  dth_join <- dth_join %>%
    mutate(!!!eval(parse(text = set_values)))
}

# Order dth_join according to order_main
for (v in order_main_vars) {
  if (v %in% names(dth_join)) {
    dth_join <- dth_join %>% arrange(.data[[v]], .by_group = FALSE)
  }
}

# If mode_main is last/first on AVISITN + AVAL, apply row selection
group_vars <- intersect(order_main_vars, names(dth_join))
if (length(group_vars) > 0) {
  if (mode_main == "first") {
    dth_join <- dth_join %>%
      group_by(across(all_of(group_vars))) %>%
      slice(1L) %>%
      ungroup()
  } else if (mode_main == "last") {
    dth_join <- dth_join %>%
      group_by(across(all_of(group_vars))) %>%
      slice(n()) %>%
      ungroup()
  }
}

# Merge back with ADSL if there is a key overlap (e.g., USUBJID)
common_keys <- intersect(names(adsl), names(dth_join))
if (length(common_keys) > 0) {
  result <- adsl %>%
    left_join(dth_join, by = common_keys)
} else {
  result <- dplyr::bind_cols(adsl, dth_join)
}

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

Create R script to perform extend condition using the admiral clinical task contract.

Input: cond.tsv, is.tsv, var.tsv
Output: result.csv

Read files from inputs/ and write the required files under outputs/.

## Inputs preview (no reference answers)

### cond.tsv (24 bytes)

### is.tsv (24 bytes)
is

### var.tsv (16 bytes)
by_vars

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(admiral)

# Input paths
cond_path <- file.path("inputs", "cond.tsv")
is_path   <- file.path("inputs", "is.tsv")
var_path  <- file.path("inputs", "var.tsv")

# Output path
out_path  <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read inputs
cond_df <- readr::read_tsv(cond_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
is_df   <- readr::read_tsv(is_path,   show_col_types = FALSE)
var_df  <- readr::read_tsv(var_path,  show_col_types = FALSE)

# Extract condition (single string)
cond_chr <- cond_df$cond[[1]]

# Extract "is" variables (character vector)
is_vars <- is_df$is

# Extract by variables (character vector)
by_vars <- var_df$by_vars

# Build condition expression
cond_expr <- rlang::parse_expr(cond_chr)

# Build dplyr expression: if condition then is_vars else NA
# result columns are named as in is_vars
mutate_calls <- purrr::map(is_vars, function(v) {
  rlang::quo(!!rlang::sym(v) := dplyr::if_else(!!cond_expr,
                                               !!rlang::sym(v),
                                               NA_real_))
})

# Create template data with all needed variables
all_vars <- unique(c(by_vars, is_vars, all.vars(cond_expr)))
adsl <- tibble::tibble(!!!setNames(rep(list(NA_real_), length(all_vars)), all_vars))

# Apply extension using mutate with dynamic calls
res <- adsl %>%
  dplyr::group_by(dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(by_vars))) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(!!!mutate_calls) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# Write result
readr::write_csv(res, out_path)
```

```r
# extract_duplicates.R

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Input paths
by_vars_path <- file.path("inputs", "by_vars.tsv")
dataset_path <- file.path("inputs", "dataset.tsv")

# Output path
out_path <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read by_vars (single column with variable names)
by_vars <- read_tsv(
  by_vars_path,
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
) %>%
  pull(by_vars) %>%
  # Remove potential blanks
  discard(~ .x == "" | is.na(.x))

# Read main dataset
dat <- read_tsv(
  dataset_path,
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# Ensure by_vars exist in dataset
by_vars <- intersect(by_vars, names(dat))

# Identify duplicates using admiral-like approach:
#  - group by by_vars
#  - keep only groups with n() > 1
res <- dat %>%
  group_by(across(all_of(by_vars))) %>%
  filter(n() > 1) %>%
  ungroup()

# Create outputs directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Write result as CSV
write_csv(res, out_path, na = "")
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(rlang)
library(admiral)

# Input/output paths
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Helper to read single-column TSV and return the non-header values
read_single_col <- function(file) {
  df <- read_tsv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
  setdiff(unlist(df[[1]]), df[[1]][1])
}

# Read control files
condition_raw      <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "condition.tsv"))
dataset_name_raw   <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "dataset_name.tsv"))
description_raw    <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "description.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
keep_source_raw    <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "keep_source_vars.tsv"))
mode_raw           <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "mode.tsv"))
order_raw          <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "order.tsv"))
set_values_to_raw  <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "set_values_to.tsv"))
set_values_to_df_raw <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "set_values_to_df.tsv"))

# Dataset name
dataset_name <- dataset_name_raw[1]

# Read input dataset (assume .tsv)
data_path_tsv <- file.path(in_dir, paste0(dataset_name, ".tsv"))
data_path_csv <- file.path(in_dir, paste0(dataset_name, ".csv"))
if (file.exists(data_path_tsv)) {
  dat <- read_tsv(data_path_tsv, show_col_types = FALSE)
} else if (file.exists(data_path_csv)) {
  dat <- read_csv(data_path_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
} else {
  stop("Input dataset not found: ", dataset_name, ".tsv or .csv")
}

# Prepare parameters

# Condition: single expression string like "AVISITN >= 0"
condition_expr <- if (length(condition_raw) > 0) condition_raw[1] else NULL
filter_expr <- if (!is.null(condition_expr) && nzchar(condition_expr)) parse_expr(condition_expr) else NULL

# Mode: e.g. "last" or "first"
mode_val <- if (length(mode_raw) > 0) mode_raw[1] else "last"

# Order variable(s)
order_vars <- order_raw[order_raw != ""]

# Description vars (grouping keys)
descr_vars <- description_raw$description
descr_vars <- descr_vars[descr_vars != ""]

# Keep source vars
keep_source_vars <- keep_source_raw[keep_source_raw != ""]

# set_values_to and set_values_to_df are expressions like
# "exprs(AVAL = mean(AVAL, na.rm=TRUE))"
# Evaluate them to rlang::quosures lists
eval_exprs_str <- function(x) {
  x <- x[x != ""]
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NULL)
  # Use first line only
  expr_str <- x[1]
  eval(parse(text = expr_str))
}

set_values_to    <- eval_exprs_str(set_values_to_raw)
set_values_to_df <- eval_exprs_str(set_values_to_df_raw)

# Build arguments for admiral::derive_var_extreme()
# Grouping vars
by_vars <- if (length(descr_vars) > 0) syms(descr_vars) else NULL

# Order by vars
order_exprs <- if (length(order_vars) > 0) syms(order_vars) else NULL

# Mode
mode_arg <- mode_val

# Filter
filter_arg <- filter_expr

# Call derive_var_extreme
# Note: set_values_to_df is used for summary data frame-level derivations
res <- derive_var_extreme(
  dataset      = dat,
  by_vars      = by_vars,
  order        = order_exprs,
  mode         = mode_arg,
  filter       = filter_arg,
  set_values_to    = set_values_to,
  set_values_to_df = set_values_to_df,
  keep_source_vars = keep_source_vars
)

# Write result
write_csv(res, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(rlang)
library(admiral)

# Input/output paths
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Helper to read single-column TSV and return the non-header values
read_single_col <- function(file) {
  df <- read_tsv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
  setdiff(unlist(df[[1]]), df[[1]][1])
}

# Read control files
condition_raw      <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "condition.tsv"))
dataset_name_raw   <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "dataset_name.tsv"))
description_raw    <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "description.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
keep_source_raw    <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "keep_source_vars.tsv"))
mode_raw           <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "mode.tsv"))
order_raw          <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "order.tsv"))
set_values_to_raw  <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "set_values_to.tsv"))
set_values_to_df_raw <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "set_values_to_df.tsv"))

# Dataset name
dataset_name <- dataset_name_raw[1]

# Read input dataset (assume .tsv)
data_path_tsv <- file.path(in_dir, paste0(dataset_name, ".tsv"))
data_path_csv <- file.path(in_dir, paste0(dataset_name, ".csv"))
if (file.exists(data_path_tsv)) {
  dat <- read_tsv(data_path_tsv, show_col_types = FALSE)
} else if (file.exists(data_path_csv)) {
  dat <- read_csv(data_path_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
} else {
  stop("Input dataset not found: ", dataset_name, ".tsv or .csv")
}

# Prepare parameters

# Condition: single expression string like "AVISITN >= 0"
condition_expr <- if (length(condition_raw) > 0) condition_raw[1] else NULL
filter_expr <- if (!is.null(condition_expr) && nzchar(condition_expr)) parse_expr(condition_expr) else NULL

# Mode: e.g. "last" or "first"
mode_val <- if (length(mode_raw) > 0) mode_raw[1] else "last"

# Order variable(s)
order_vars <- order_raw[order_raw != ""]

# Description vars (grouping keys)
descr_vars <- description_raw$description
descr_vars <- descr_vars[descr_vars != ""]

# Keep source vars
keep_source_vars <- keep_source_raw[keep_source_raw != ""]

# set_values_to and set_values_to_df are expressions like
# "exprs(AVAL = mean(AVAL, na.rm=TRUE))"
# Evaluate them to rlang::quosures lists
eval_exprs_str <- function(x) {
  x <- x[x != ""]
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NULL)
  # Use first line only
  expr_str <- x[1]
  eval(parse(text = expr_str))
}

set_values_to    <- eval_exprs_str(set_values_to_raw)
set_values_to_df <- eval_exprs_str(set_values_to_df_raw)

# Build arguments for admiral::derive_var_extreme()
# Grouping vars
by_vars <- if (length(descr_vars) > 0) syms(descr_vars) else NULL

# Order by vars
order_exprs <- if (length(order_vars) > 0) syms(order_vars) else NULL

# Mode
mode_arg <- mode_val

# Filter
filter_arg <- filter_expr

# Call derive_var_extreme
# Note: set_values_to_df is used for summary data frame-level derivations
res <- derive_var_extreme(
  dataset      = dat,
  by_vars      = by_vars,
  order        = order_exprs,
  mode         = mode_arg,
  filter       = filter_arg,
  set_values_to    = set_values_to,
  set_values_to_df = set_values_to_df,
  keep_source_vars = keep_source_vars
)

# Write result
write_csv(res, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")

```r
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(admiral)

# Input paths
by_vars_path <- file.path("inputs", "by_vars.tsv")
source_path  <- file.path("inputs", "source_datasets.tsv")

# Output directory
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read inputs
by_vars <- read_tsv(by_vars_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
source_datasets <- read_tsv(source_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Build by_vars expression list for derive_vars_merged/extend
by_syms <- syms(by_vars$by_vars)

# Perform extend source datasets using admiral clinical task contract:
# Create all combinations of BY variables within each USUBJID/STUDYID/PARAMCD
result <- source_datasets %>%
  group_by(USUBJID, STUDYID, !!!by_syms, PARAMCD) %>%
  tidyr::nest() %>%
  group_by(USUBJID, STUDYID, PARAMCD) %>%
  tidyr::complete(!!!by_syms) %>%
  left_join(
    source_datasets,
    by = c("USUBJID", "STUDYID", "PARAMCD", by_vars$by_vars)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

library(dplyr)

source_path  <- file.path("inputs", "source_datasets.tsv")

by_syms <- syms(by_vars$by_vars)

result <- source_datasets %>%

  group_by(USUBJID, STUDYID, PARAMCD) %>%
  tidyr::complete(!!!by_syms) %>%
  left_join(
    source_datasets,
    by = c("USUBJID", "STUDYID", "PARAMCD", by_vars$by_vars)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Write result

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(admiral)
library(admiraldev)
library(stringr)

# Create outputs dir if not exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Helper to read single-column TSV and return vector (without header)
read_single_tsv <- function(path) {
  df <- read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  # Return non-NA, non-empty values from first column
  vals <- df[[1]]
  vals[!is.na(vals) & vals != ""]
}

# Read control inputs
condition_vec      <- read_single_tsv("inputs/condition.tsv")
dataset_name_vec   <- read_single_tsv("inputs/dataset_name.tsv")
first_cond_lower   <- read_single_tsv("inputs/first_cond_lower.tsv")
first_cond_upper   <- read_single_tsv("inputs/first_cond_upper.tsv")
join_type_vec      <- read_single_tsv("inputs/join_type.tsv")
join_vars_vec      <- read_single_tsv("inputs/join_vars.tsv")
order_vec          <- read_single_tsv("inputs/order.tsv")

condition      <- condition_vec[1]
dataset_name   <- dataset_name_vec[1]
first_cond_low <- first_cond_lower[1]
first_cond_up  <- first_cond_upper[1]
join_type      <- join_type_vec[1]

# Parse join_vars and order variables (assume comma- or space-separated single var names)
parse_var_list <- function(x) {
  if (length(x) == 0 || all(is.na(x))) return(character())
  x <- x[1]
  x <- gsub("[,;]", " ", x)
  vars <- unlist(strsplit(x, "\\s+"))
  vars[vars != ""]
}

join_vars <- parse_var_list(join_vars_vec)
order_vars <- parse_var_list(order_vec)

# Load input dataset (assume TSV with name {dataset_name}.tsv in inputs/)
data_path <- file.path("inputs", paste0(dataset_name, ".tsv"))
adsl_path <- file.path("inputs", "adsl.tsv")

if (!file.exists(data_path)) {
  stop(paste("Dataset file not found:", data_path))
}
if (!file.exists(adsl_path)) {
  stop("ADSL dataset (adsl.tsv) not found in inputs/")
}

adae <- read_tsv(data_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
adsl <- read_tsv(adsl_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Ensure admiral expects lower case column names if needed
# Here we keep original names as in inputs

# Convert strings to expressions for filter conditions
parse_cond <- function(cond_str) {
  if (is.na(cond_str) || cond_str == "") return(rlang::expr(TRUE))
  rlang::parse_expr(cond_str)
}

cond_expr        <- parse_cond(condition)
first_low_expr   <- parse_cond(first_cond_low)
first_up_expr    <- parse_cond(first_cond_up)

# Build unquoted variable lists for admiral
join_vars_syms <- rlang::syms(join_vars)
order_syms     <- rlang::syms(order_vars)

# Derive EVENTJOIN like object using admiral::derive_event_joined()
# Assume source dataset 'adae' represents events; join to ADSL by USUBJID if present
if (!"USUBJID" %in% names(adae) || !"USUBJID" %in% names(adsl)) {
  stop("Both input dataset and ADSL must contain USUBJID.")
}

# Prepare dataset according to admiral expectations
# The event join is typically performed on a time variable; use AVISITN as a proxy here
if (!"AVISITN" %in% names(adae)) {
  stop("Input dataset must contain AVISITN variable for this task.")
}

# Derive joined events
res <- derive_event_joined(
  dataset = adae,
  by_vars = exprs(USUBJID),
  order = !!!order_syms,
  first_cond_low = !!first_low_expr,
  first_cond_up  = !!first_up_expr,
  join_vars      = !!!join_vars_syms,
  join_type      = join_type
) %>%
  filter(!!cond_expr)

# Write result
write_csv(res, file = "outputs/result.csv", na = "")
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(admiral)
library(admiraldev)
library(stringr)

# Create outputs dir if not exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Helper to read single-column TSV and return vector (without header)
read_single_tsv <- function(path) {
  df <- read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  # Return non-NA, non-empty values from first column
  vals <- df[[1]]
  vals[!is.na(vals) & vals != ""]
}

# Read control inputs
condition_vec      <- read_single_tsv("inputs/condition.tsv")
dataset_name_vec   <- read_single_tsv("inputs/dataset_name.tsv")
first_cond_lower   <- read_single_tsv("inputs/first_cond_lower.tsv")
first_cond_upper   <- read_single_tsv("inputs/first_cond_upper.tsv")
join_type_vec      <- read_single_tsv("inputs/join_type.tsv")
join_vars_vec      <- read_single_tsv("inputs/join_vars.tsv")
order_vec          <- read_single_tsv("inputs/order.tsv")

condition      <- condition_vec[1]
dataset_name   <- dataset_name_vec[1]
first_cond_low <- first_cond_lower[1]
first_cond_up  <- first_cond_upper[1]
join_type      <- join_type_vec[1]

# Parse join_vars and order variables (assume comma- or space-separated single var names)
parse_var_list <- function(x) {
  if (length(x) == 0 || all(is.na(x))) return(character())
  x <- x[1]
  x <- gsub("[,;]", " ", x)
  vars <- unlist(strsplit(x, "\\s+"))
  vars[vars != ""]
}

join_vars <- parse_var_list(join_vars_vec)
order_vars <- parse_var_list(order_vec)

# Load input dataset (assume TSV with name {dataset_name}.tsv in inputs/)
data_path <- file.path("inputs", paste0(dataset_name, ".tsv"))
adsl_path <- file.path("inputs", "adsl.tsv")

if (!file.exists(data_path)) {
  stop(paste("Dataset file not found:", data_path))
}
if (!file.exists(adsl_path)) {
  stop("ADSL dataset (adsl.tsv) not found in inputs/")
}

adae <- read_tsv(data_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
adsl <- read_tsv(adsl_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Ensure admiral expects lower case column names if needed
# Here we keep original names as in inputs

# Convert strings to expressions for filter conditions
parse_cond <- function(cond_str) {
  if (is.na(cond_str) || cond_str == "") return(rlang::expr(TRUE))
  rlang::parse_expr(cond_str)
}

cond_expr        <- parse_cond(condition)
first_low_expr   <- parse_cond(first_cond_low)
first_up_expr    <- parse_cond(first_cond_up)

# Build unquoted variable lists for admiral
join_vars_syms <- rlang::syms(join_vars)
order_syms     <- rlang::syms(order_vars)

# Derive EVENTJOIN like object using admiral::derive_event_joined()
# Assume source dataset 'adae' represents events; join to ADSL by USUBJID if present
if (!"USUBJID" %in% names(adae) || !"USUBJID" %in% names(adsl)) {
  stop("Both input dataset and ADSL must contain USUBJID.")
}

# Prepare dataset according to admiral expectations
# The event join is typically performed on a time variable; use AVISITN as a proxy here
if (!"AVISITN" %in% names(adae)) {
  stop("Input dataset must contain AVISITN variable for this task.")
}

# Derive joined events
res <- derive_event_joined(
  dataset = adae,
  by_vars = exprs(USUBJID),
  order = !!!order_syms,
  first_cond_low = !!first_low_expr,
  first_cond_up  = !!first_up_expr,
  join_vars      = !!!join_vars_syms,
  join_type      = join_type
) %>%
  filter(!!cond_expr)

# Write result
write_csv(res, file = "outputs/result.csv", na = "")

```r
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Input paths
by_vars_path    <- file.path("inputs", "by_vars.tsv")
dataset_path    <- file.path("inputs", "dataset.tsv")
dataset_add_path<- file.path("inputs", "dataset_add.tsv")
filter_add_path <- file.path("inputs", "filter_add.tsv")

# Output path
out_path <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read inputs
by_vars <- read_tsv(by_vars_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset <- read_tsv(dataset_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset_add <- read_tsv(dataset_add_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
filter_add_df <- read_tsv(filter_add_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Extract by variables and filter expression
by_vars_vec <- by_vars[[1]]
filter_expr <- filter_add_df[[1]][1]

# Apply filter to dataset_add
dataset_add_filt <- dataset_add %>%
  filter(!!rlang::parse_expr(filter_expr))

# Perform "filter exist" using admiral-like contract:
# keep rows in dataset where a matching record exists in filtered dataset_add
result <- dataset %>%
  semi_join(dataset_add_filt, by = by_vars_vec)

# Ensure outputs directory exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Write result
write_csv(result, out_path, na = "")
```

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Input paths
by_vars_path    <- file.path("inputs", "by_vars.tsv")
dataset_path    <- file.path("inputs", "dataset.tsv")
dataset_add_path<- file.path("inputs", "dataset_add.tsv")
filter_add_path <- file.path("inputs", "filter_add.tsv")

# Output path
out_path <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read inputs
by_vars <- read_tsv(by_vars_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset <- read_tsv(dataset_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset_add <- read_tsv(dataset_add_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
filter_add_df <- read_tsv(filter_add_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Extract by variables and filter expression
by_vars_vec <- by_vars[[1]]
filter_expr <- filter_add_df[[1]][1]

# Apply filter to dataset_add
dataset_add_filt <- dataset_add %>%
  filter(!!rlang::parse_expr(filter_expr))

# Perform "filter exist" using admiral-like contract:
# keep rows in dataset where a matching record exists in filtered dataset_add
result <- dataset %>%
  semi_join(dataset_add_filt, by = by_vars_vec)

# Ensure outputs directory exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Write result
write_csv(result, out_path, na = "")

```r
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(admiral)

# Input paths
in_dir <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

# Read control files
by_vars <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "by_vars.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  pull(by_vars)

check_type <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "check_type.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  pull(check_type) %>%
  dplyr::first()

mode_val <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "mode.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  pull(mode) %>%
  dplyr::first()

order_var <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "order.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  pull(order) %>%
  dplyr::first()

# Read main dataset
dataset <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "dataset.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# Build quosures for admiral::filter_extreme()
by_syms <- rlang::syms(by_vars)
order_sym <- rlang::sym(order_var)

# Map mode string to admiral direction argument
# "first" -> "first", "last" -> "last", etc.
direction <- mode_val

# Apply filter_extreme
result <- admiral::filter_extreme(
  dataset,
  by_vars = by_syms,
  order = exprs(!!order_sym),
  mode = direction,
  check_type = check_type
)

# Ensure outputs directory exists
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

library(dplyr)

library(admiral)

# Input paths
in_dir <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

# Read control files
by_vars <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "by_vars.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  pull(by_vars)

check_type <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "check_type.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  pull(check_type) %>%
  dplyr::first()

mode_val <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "mode.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  pull(mode) %>%
  dplyr::first()

order_var <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "order.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  pull(order) %>%
  dplyr::first()

# Read main dataset
dataset <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "dataset.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# Build quosures for admiral::filter_extreme()
by_syms <- rlang::syms(by_vars)
order_sym <- rlang::sym(order_var)

# Map mode string to admiral direction argument
# "first" -> "first", "last" -> "last", etc.
direction <- mode_val

# Apply filter_extreme
result <- admiral::filter_extreme(
  dataset,
  by_vars = by_syms,
  order = exprs(!!order_sym),
  mode = direction,
  check_type = check_type
)

# Ensure outputs directory exists
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# create outputs dir if not exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#-----------------------------
# Read task contract inputs
#-----------------------------
by_vars <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "by_vars.tsv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  pull(1) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  as.character()

cond_raw <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "condition.tsv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  pull(1) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  paste(collapse = " ")

dataset_name <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "dataset_name.tsv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  pull(1) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  as.character() %>%
  .[1]

#-----------------------------
# Read main dataset
#-----------------------------
data_path_rds <- file.path("inputs", paste0(dataset_name, ".rds"))
data_path_tsv <- file.path("inputs", paste0(dataset_name, ".tsv"))
data_path_csv <- file.path("inputs", paste0(dataset_name, ".csv"))

if (file.exists(data_path_rds)) {
  dat <- readRDS(data_path_rds)
} else if (file.exists(data_path_tsv)) {
  dat <- read_tsv(data_path_tsv, show_col_types = FALSE)
} else if (file.exists(data_path_csv)) {
  dat <- read_csv(data_path_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
} else {
  stop("No input dataset found for name: ", dataset_name)
}

#-----------------------------
# Apply flag condition within by_vars
#-----------------------------
if (length(by_vars) == 0) {
  dat_flagged <- dat %>%
    mutate(FLAG = !!rlang::parse_expr(cond_raw))
} else {
  dat_flagged <- dat %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(by_vars))) %>%
    mutate(FLAG = !!rlang::parse_expr(cond_raw)) %>%
    ungroup()
}

#-----------------------------
# Write result
#-----------------------------
write_csv(dat_flagged, file.path("outputs", "result.csv"), na = "")
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# create outputs dir if not exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#-----------------------------
# Read task contract inputs
#-----------------------------
by_vars <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "by_vars.tsv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  pull(1) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  as.character()

cond_raw <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "condition.tsv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  pull(1) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  paste(collapse = " ")

dataset_name <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "dataset_name.tsv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  pull(1) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  as.character() %>%
  .[1]

#-----------------------------
# Read main dataset
#-----------------------------
data_path_rds <- file.path("inputs", paste0(dataset_name, ".rds"))
data_path_tsv <- file.path("inputs", paste0(dataset_name, ".tsv"))
data_path_csv <- file.path("inputs", paste0(dataset_name, ".csv"))

if (file.exists(data_path_rds)) {
  dat <- readRDS(data_path_rds)
} else if (file.exists(data_path_tsv)) {
  dat <- read_tsv(data_path_tsv, show_col_types = FALSE)
} else if (file.exists(data_path_csv)) {
  dat <- read_csv(data_path_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
} else {
  stop("No input dataset found for name: ", dataset_name)
}

#-----------------------------
# Apply flag condition within by_vars
#-----------------------------
if (length(by_vars) == 0) {
  dat_flagged <- dat %>%
    mutate(FLAG = !!rlang::parse_expr(cond_raw))
} else {
  dat_flagged <- dat %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(by_vars))) %>%
    mutate(FLAG = !!rlang::parse_expr(cond_raw)) %>%
    ungroup()
}

#-----------------------------
# Write result
#-----------------------------
write_csv(dat_flagged, file.path("outputs", "result.csv"), na = "")

```r
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Input/output dirs
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Helper to read 1-col tsv as vector
read_single_col <- function(file, colname) {
  df <- read_tsv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
  df[[colname]][!is.na(df[[colname]]) & df[[colname]] != ""]
}

# Read control files
by_vars    <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "by_vars.tsv"),    "by_vars")
check_type <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "check_type.tsv"), "check_type")
join_vars  <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "join_vars.tsv"),  "join_vars")
tmp_obs    <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "tmp_obs_nr_var.tsv"), "tmp_obs_nr_var")

filter_add_expr   <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "filter_add.tsv"),   show_col_types = FALSE)$filter[1]
filter_join_expr  <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "filter_join.tsv"),  show_col_types = FALSE)$filter[1]
first_low_expr    <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "first_cond_lower.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)$cond[1]
first_up_expr     <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "first_cond_upper.tsv"),  show_col_types = FALSE)$cond[1]
order_expr        <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "order.tsv"),        show_col_types = FALSE)$order[1]

# Read datasets
dataset     <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "dataset.tsv"),     show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset_add <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "dataset_add.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# 1) Apply filter_add to dataset_add
dataset_add_f <- dataset_add %>%
  filter(eval(parse(text = filter_add_expr)))

# 2) Create temporary observation number within by_vars + tmp_obs grouping
#    using dataset_add_f and first_cond_* conditions
dataset_add_tmp <- dataset_add_f %>%
  filter(eval(parse(text = first_low_expr)),
         eval(parse(text = first_up_expr))) %>%
  group_by(across(all_of(c(by_vars, tmp_obs)))) %>%
  mutate(__TMP_OBS_NR__ = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()

# 3) Join dataset and dataset_add_tmp by by_vars + join_vars + tmp_obs
join_by <- unique(c(by_vars, join_vars, tmp_obs))

joined <- dataset %>%
  inner_join(dataset_add_tmp, by = join_by,
             suffix = c("", "_ADD")) %>%
  # 4) Apply filter_join on joined data
  filter(eval(parse(text = filter_join_expr)))

# 5) Apply ordering
order_parts <- str_split(order_expr, ",")[[1]] %>%
  str_trim()
# build arrange call programmatically
arranged <- joined %>%
  arrange(across(all_of(order_parts)))

# 6) Write result
write_csv(arranged, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Input/output dirs
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Helper to read 1-col tsv as vector
read_single_col <- function(file, colname) {
  df <- read_tsv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
  df[[colname]][!is.na(df[[colname]]) & df[[colname]] != ""]
}

# Read control files
by_vars    <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "by_vars.tsv"),    "by_vars")
check_type <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "check_type.tsv"), "check_type")
join_vars  <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "join_vars.tsv"),  "join_vars")
tmp_obs    <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "tmp_obs_nr_var.tsv"), "tmp_obs_nr_var")

filter_add_expr   <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "filter_add.tsv"),   show_col_types = FALSE)$filter[1]
filter_join_expr  <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "filter_join.tsv"),  show_col_types = FALSE)$filter[1]
first_low_expr    <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "first_cond_lower.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)$cond[1]
first_up_expr     <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "first_cond_upper.tsv"),  show_col_types = FALSE)$cond[1]
order_expr        <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "order.tsv"),        show_col_types = FALSE)$order[1]

# Read datasets
dataset     <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "dataset.tsv"),     show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset_add <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "dataset_add.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# 1) Apply filter_add to dataset_add
dataset_add_f <- dataset_add %>%
  filter(eval(parse(text = filter_add_expr)))

# 2) Create temporary observation number within by_vars + tmp_obs grouping
#    using dataset_add_f and first_cond_* conditions
dataset_add_tmp <- dataset_add_f %>%
  filter(eval(parse(text = first_low_expr)),
         eval(parse(text = first_up_expr))) %>%
  group_by(across(all_of(c(by_vars, tmp_obs)))) %>%
  mutate(__TMP_OBS_NR__ = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()

# 3) Join dataset and dataset_add_tmp by by_vars + join_vars + tmp_obs
join_by <- unique(c(by_vars, join_vars, tmp_obs))

joined <- dataset %>%
  inner_join(dataset_add_tmp, by = join_by,
             suffix = c("", "_ADD")) %>%
  # 4) Apply filter_join on joined data
  filter(eval(parse(text = filter_join_expr)))

# 5) Apply ordering
order_parts <- str_split(order_expr, ",")[[1]] %>%
  str_trim()
# build arrange call programmatically
arranged <- joined %>%
  arrange(across(all_of(order_parts)))

# 6) Write result
write_csv(arranged, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)

inputs_dir <- "inputs"
outputs_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(outputs_dir)) dir.create(outputs_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read parameter files ----------------------------------------------------

by_vars_df <- read_tsv(file.path(inputs_dir, "by_vars.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
check_type_df <- read_tsv(file.path(inputs_dir, "check_type.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
condition_df <- read_tsv(file.path(inputs_dir, "condition.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
inclusive_df <- read_tsv(file.path(inputs_dir, "inclusive.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
keep_no_ref_groups_df <- read_tsv(file.path(inputs_dir, "keep_no_ref_groups.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
mode_df <- read_tsv(file.path(inputs_dir, "mode.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
order_df <- read_tsv(file.path(inputs_dir, "order.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
selection_df <- read_tsv(file.path(inputs_dir, "selection.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

by_vars <- syms(by_vars_df$by_vars)
check_type <- check_type_df$check_type[1]
cond_expr <- parse_expr(condition_df$cond[1])
inclusive_vars <- selection_df$selection
keep_no_ref_groups_vars <- keep_no_ref_groups_df$keep_no_ref_groups
mode_val <- mode_df$mode[1]
order_exprs <- syms(order_df$order)
selection_vars <- selection_df$selection

# Read dataset ------------------------------------------------------------

adsl_raw <- read_tsv(
  file.path(inputs_dir, "dataset.tsv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  na = c("", "NA")
)

adsl <- adsl_raw %>%
  mutate(
    ADT = as.Date(ADT)
  )

# Build arguments for filter_relative -------------------------------------

ref_cond <- cond_expr

order_ref <- quo(NULL)
order_target <- exprs(!!!order_exprs)

filter_mode <- mode_val

inclusive <- inclusive_vars
keep_no_ref_groups <- keep_no_ref_groups_vars

# Apply filter_relative ---------------------------------------------------

result <- filter_relative(
  dataset = adsl,
  by_vars = !!!by_vars,
  condition = !!ref_cond,
  mode = filter_mode,
  order = order_target,
  check_type = check_type,
  inclusive = all_of(inclusive),
  keep_no_ref_groups = all_of(keep_no_ref_groups)
)

# Keep only requested selection variables ---------------------------------

result_out <- result %>%
  select(all_of(selection_vars))

# Write output ------------------------------------------------------------

write_csv(result_out, file.path(outputs_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(admiral)

# Input / output paths
in_file  <- file.path("inputs", "option.tsv")
out_file <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read options
opt_df <- read_tsv(in_file, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Extract option vector
opts <- opt_df$option

# Get current admiral options
all_opts <- get_admiral_option()

# Build result table
res <- tibble(
  option = opts,
  value  = sapply(opts, function(x) {
    if (x %in% names(all_opts)) {
      val <- all_opts[[x]]
      if (length(val) > 1) paste(val, collapse = ",") else as.character(val)
    } else {
      NA_character_
    }
  })
)

# Ensure outputs directory exists
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Write result
write_csv(res, out_file)
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read imputed DTC-like data (wide format with header names in file)
imp_raw <- read_tsv(
  file.path(in_dir, "imputed.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# Ensure expected columns exist (AVAL, AVISITN, USUBJID, DTC)
# If DTC not present, attempt to detect/construct; here we just keep present cols
needed <- c("USUBJID", "AVISITN", "AVAL", "DTC")
present <- intersect(needed, names(imp_raw))
imp <- imp_raw %>% select(all_of(present))

# Example admiral-style DTC formatting:
# - Standardize date-like column DTC
# - Derive analysis date (ADT) and analysis day (ADY) if DTC exists
res <- imp %>%
  mutate(
    DTC = if_else(!is.na(DTC) & str_trim(DTC) == "", NA_character_, DTC),
    # Impute partial dates (e.g., yyyy-mm, yyyy) to first day/month as needed
    DTC = case_when(
      str_detect(DTC, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$") ~ DTC,
      str_detect(DTC, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}$")        ~ str_c(DTC, "-01"),
      str_detect(DTC, "^\\d{4}$")              ~ str_c(DTC, "-01-01"),
      TRUE                                     ~ DTC
    ),
    ADT = suppressWarnings(as.Date(DTC)),
    # Example reference day = first non-missing ADT per subject
    AREFDT = suppressWarnings(
      as.Date(if_else(!is.na(ADT),
                      as.character(min(ADT, na.rm = TRUE)),
                      NA_character_))
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(USUBJID) %>%
  mutate(
    AREFDT = suppressWarnings(min(ADT, na.rm = TRUE)),
    AREFDT = if_else(is.infinite(AREFDT), as.Date(NA_character_), AREFDT),
    ADY = if_else(!is.na(ADT) & !is.na(AREFDT),
                  as.integer(ADT - AREFDT) + 1L,
                  NA_integer_)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(any_of(c("USUBJID", "AVISITN", "AVAL", "DTC", "ADT", "ADY")))

# Write result
write_csv(res, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read imputed DTC-like data (wide format with header names in file)
imp_raw <- read_tsv(
  file.path(in_dir, "imputed.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# Ensure expected columns exist (AVAL, AVISITN, USUBJID, DTC)
# If DTC not present, attempt to detect/construct; here we just keep present cols
needed <- c("USUBJID", "AVISITN", "AVAL", "DTC")
present <- intersect(needed, names(imp_raw))
imp <- imp_raw %>% select(all_of(present))

# Example admiral-style DTC formatting:
# - Standardize date-like column DTC
# - Derive analysis date (ADT) and analysis day (ADY) if DTC exists
res <- imp %>%
  mutate(
    DTC = if_else(!is.na(DTC) & str_trim(DTC) == "", NA_character_, DTC),
    # Impute partial dates (e.g., yyyy-mm, yyyy) to first day/month as needed
    DTC = case_when(
      str_detect(DTC, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$") ~ DTC,
      str_detect(DTC, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}$")        ~ str_c(DTC, "-01"),
      str_detect(DTC, "^\\d{4}$")              ~ str_c(DTC, "-01-01"),
      TRUE                                     ~ DTC
    ),
    ADT = suppressWarnings(as.Date(DTC)),
    # Example reference day = first non-missing ADT per subject
    AREFDT = suppressWarnings(
      as.Date(if_else(!is.na(ADT),
                      as.character(min(ADT, na.rm = TRUE)),
                      NA_character_))
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(USUBJID) %>%
  mutate(
    AREFDT = suppressWarnings(min(ADT, na.rm = TRUE)),
    AREFDT = if_else(is.infinite(AREFDT), as.Date(NA_character_), AREFDT),
    ADY = if_else(!is.na(ADT) & !is.na(AREFDT),
                  as.integer(ADT - AREFDT) + 1L,
                  NA_integer_)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(any_of(c("USUBJID", "AVISITN", "AVAL", "DTC", "ADT", "ADY")))

# Write result
write_csv(res, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")

```r
filter_df  <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "filter.tsv"),    show_col_types = FALSE)
```

# Input/output paths

out_dir <- "outputs"

# Create outputs directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read inputs
cond_df    <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "condition.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
filter_df  <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "filter.tsv"),    show_col_types = FALSE)
newvar_df  <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "new_var.tsv"),   show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset_df <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "dataset.tsv"),   show_col_types = FALSE)

# Extract strings
cond_str   <- cond_df$cond[1]
filter_str <- filter_df$filter[1]
new_var    <- newvar_df$new_var[1]

# Build expressions
cond_expr   <- parse_expr(cond_str)
filter_expr <- parse_expr(filter_str)

# Flag records
result <- dataset_df %>%
  mutate(
    !!new_var := case_when(
      !!filter_expr & !!cond_expr ~ "Y",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# Write output
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Paths
in_file  <- file.path("inputs", "time_imputation.tsv")
out_file <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read input
df <- read_tsv(in_file, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Function to derive imputation target time per ADaM guidelines
# Here we assume the provided time is already the target imputation time;
# parse and reformat to HH:MM:SS
derive_imputation_time <- function(x) {
  x <- str_trim(x)
  if (x == "" | is.na(x)) return(NA_character_)
  # basic validation / normalization
  t <- str_split_fixed(x, ":", 3)
  if (ncol(t) != 3) return(NA_character_)
  hh <- sprintf("%02d", as.integer(t[, 1]))
  mm <- sprintf("%02d", as.integer(t[, 2]))
  ss <- sprintf("%02d", as.integer(t[, 3]))
  paste0(hh, ":", mm, ":", ss)
}

result <- df %>%
  mutate(
    imputation_target_time = derive_imputation_time(time_imputation)
  )

# Write output
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
write_csv(result, out_file)
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Paths
in_file  <- file.path("inputs", "time_imputation.tsv")
out_file <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read input
df <- read_tsv(in_file, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Function to derive imputation target time per ADaM guidelines
# Here we assume the provided time is already the target imputation time;
# parse and reformat to HH:MM:SS
derive_imputation_time <- function(x) {
  x <- str_trim(x)
  if (x == "" | is.na(x)) return(NA_character_)
  # basic validation / normalization
  t <- str_split_fixed(x, ":", 3)
  if (ncol(t) != 3) return(NA_character_)
  hh <- sprintf("%02d", as.integer(t[, 1]))
  mm <- sprintf("%02d", as.integer(t[, 2]))
  ss <- sprintf("%02d", as.integer(t[, 3]))
  paste0(hh, ":", mm, ":", ss)
}

result <- df %>%
  mutate(
    imputation_target_time = derive_imputation_time(time_imputation)
  )

# Write output
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
write_csv(result, out_file)

month

raw_response:
```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read inputs
date_imp_cfg <- read_tsv(
  file.path(in_dir, "date_imputation.tsv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

month <- read_tsv(
  file.path(in_dir, "month.tsv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

# Expecting admiral date imputation type in first row / column `date_imputation`
imp_type <- date_imp_cfg$date_imputation[[1]]

# Helper to impute a date from a partial ISO string and type
impute_date <- function(x, type) {
  # x is character vector of dates like "2020-02-  ", "2020-  -  ", etc.
  # type is one of "FIRST", "MIDDLE", "LAST"
  x_chr <- as.character(x)

  # Extract components when present
  yr  <- str_sub(x_chr, 1, 4)
  mo  <- str_sub(x_chr, 6, 7)
  dy  <- str_sub(x_chr, 9, 10)

  # If missing, set according to type
  mo_imp <- ifelse(
    str_trim(mo) == "" | is.na(mo),
    if (toupper(type) == "FIRST") "01" else if (toupper(type) == "MIDDLE") "06" else "12",
    mo
  )

  # For day, we need max day in month for LAST, mid for MIDDLE
  # First construct a temporary date with day 01 for LAST/MIDDLE to get month length
  base_date <- suppressWarnings(
    ymd(paste0(yr, "-", mo_imp, "-01"))
  )

  max_day <- ifelse(
    is.na(base_date),
    NA_integer_,
    day(ceiling_date(base_date, "month") - days(1))
  )

  dy_imp <- case_when(
    str_trim(dy) != "" & !is.na(dy) ~ dy,
    toupper(type) == "FIRST" ~ "01",
    toupper(type) == "MIDDLE" ~ sprintf("%02d", ceiling(max_day / 2)),
    toupper(type) == "LAST"   ~ sprintf("%02d", max_day),
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )

  res <- suppressWarnings(
    ymd(paste0(yr, "-", mo_imp, "-", dy_imp))
  )

  res
}

# Assume AVAL is a partial date character suitable for imputation
result <- month %>%
  mutate(
    AVALC = as.character(AVAL),
    AVAL_DT = impute_date(AVALC, imp_type)
  )

# Write output
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read inputs
date_imp_cfg <- read_tsv(
  file.path(in_dir, "date_imputation.tsv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

month <- read_tsv(
  file.path(in_dir, "month.tsv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

# Expecting admiral date imputation type in first row / column `date_imputation`
imp_type <- date_imp_cfg$date_imputation[[1]]

# Helper to impute a date from a partial ISO string and type
impute_date <- function(x, type) {
  # x is character vector of dates like "2020-02-  ", "2020-  -  ", etc.
  # type is one of "FIRST", "MIDDLE", "LAST"
  x_chr <- as.character(x)

  # Extract components when present
  yr  <- str_sub(x_chr, 1, 4)
  mo  <- str_sub(x_chr, 6, 7)
  dy  <- str_sub(x_chr, 9, 10)

  # If missing, set according to type
  mo_imp <- ifelse(
    str_trim(mo) == "" | is.na(mo),
    if (toupper(type) == "FIRST") "01" else if (toupper(type) == "MIDDLE") "06" else "12",
    mo
  )

  # For day, we need max day in month for LAST, mid for MIDDLE
  # First construct a temporary date with day 01 for LAST/MIDDLE to get month length
  base_date <- suppressWarnings(
    ymd(paste0(yr, "-", mo_imp, "-01"))
  )

  max_day <- ifelse(
    is.na(base_date),
    NA_integer_,
    day(ceiling_date(base_date, "month") - days(1))
  )

  dy_imp <- case_when(
    str_trim(dy) != "" & !is.na(dy) ~ dy,
    toupper(type) == "FIRST" ~ "01",
    toupper(type) == "MIDDLE" ~ sprintf("%02d", ceiling(max_day / 2)),
    toupper(type) == "LAST"   ~ sprintf("%02d", max_day),
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )

  res <- suppressWarnings(
    ymd(paste0(yr, "-", mo_imp, "-", dy_imp))
  )

  res
}

# Assume AVAL is a partial date character suitable for imputation
result <- month %>%
  mutate(
    AVALC = as.character(AVAL),
    AVAL_DT = impute_date(AVALC, imp_type)
  )

# Write output
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)

in_dir <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

by_vars <- read.delim(file.path(in_dir, "by_vars.tsv"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
by_vars <- setdiff(by_vars[[1]], "")

datase <- read.delim(file.path(in_dir, "datase.tsv"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

filter_expr <- read.delim(file.path(in_dir, "filter.tsv"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)[[1]]
filter_expr <- filter_expr[filter_expr != ""][1]

parameters <- read.delim(file.path(in_dir, "parameters.tsv"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)[[1]]
parameters <- parameters[parameters != "" & parameters != "parameters"]

set_values_raw <- read.delim(file.path(in_dir, "set_values_t.tsv"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)[[1]]
set_values_raw <- set_values_raw[set_values_raw != ""]
set_values_raw <- set_values_raw[grepl("^exprs\\(", set_values_raw)]
set_values_raw <- sub("^exprs\\(", "", set_values_raw)
set_values_raw <- sub("\\)$", "", set_values_raw)

set_pairs <- str_split(set_values_raw, ",")[[1]]
set_pairs <- trimws(set_pairs)
set_list <- lapply(set_pairs, function(x) {
  kv <- str_split_fixed(x, "=", 2)
  name <- trimws(kv[1])
  val <- trimws(kv[2])
  val <- sub("^'(.*)'$", "\\1", val)
  list(name = name, value = val)
})
set_values <- setNames(lapply(set_list, `[[`, "value"),
                       sapply(set_list, `[[`, "name"))

datase_filtered <- datase %>%
  filter(!!rlang::parse_expr(filter_expr)) %>%
  filter(PARAMCD %in% parameters)

hori <- datase_filtered %>%
  arrange(across(all_of(c(by_vars, "PARAMCD", "ADT")))) %>%
  group_by(across(all_of(c(by_vars, "PARAMCD")))) %>%
  tidyr::fill(AVAL, .direction = "down") %>%
  ungroup()

for (nm in names(set_values)) {
  hori[[nm]] <- set_values[[nm]]
}

write.csv(hori, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), row.names = FALSE, na = "")
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
```r
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read inputs
df_creat <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "create_datetime.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
df_dtc   <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "dtc.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# Function to convert ISO-like partial date/time (DTC) to min and max possible datetimes
# according to CDISC/ADaM-like rules
dtc_to_range <- function(dtc) {
  dtc <- str_trim(dtc)
  na  <- tibble(
    dtm_min = as.POSIXct(NA),
    dtm_max = as.POSIXct(NA)
  )

  # Empty or missing
  if (is.na(dtc) || dtc == "") return(na)

  # If contains '---' only (no usable date/time fields)
  if (!str_detect(dtc, "[0-9]")) return(na)

  # Split into date / time part
  parts <- str_split_fixed(dtc, "T", 2)
  date_part <- parts[, 1]
  time_part <- ifelse(parts[, 2] == "", NA_character_, parts[, 2])

  # Helper to build min/max date based on partialness
  get_date_range <- function(dp) {
    # Patterns (year, year-month, year-month-day) possibly with ---
    # Extract numeric year, month, day where present
    y <- str_sub(dp, 1, 4)
    m <- ifelse(str_length(dp) >= 7, str_sub(dp, 6, 7), NA_character_)
    d <- ifelse(str_length(dp) >= 10, str_sub(dp, 9, 10), NA_character_)

    # If year has missing digits treat as NA
    if (!str_detect(y, "^[0-9]{4}$")) return(list(dmin = NA_Date_, dmax = NA_Date_))

    year <- as.integer(y)

    # Year only
    if (is.na(m) || !str_detect(m, "^[0-9]{2}$")) {
      dmin <- as.Date(paste0(year, "-01-01"))
      dmax <- as.Date(paste0(year, "-12-31"))
      return(list(dmin = dmin, dmax = dmax))
    }

    month <- as.integer(m)

    # Year-month only
    if (is.na(d) || !str_detect(d, "^[0-9]{2}$")) {
      dmin <- as.Date(paste0(year, "-", sprintf("%02d", month), "-01"))
      dmax <- ceiling_date(dmin, "month") - days(1)
      return(list(dmin = dmin, dmax = dmax))
    }

    # Full date
    day <- as.integer(d)
    # Validate date; if invalid, NA
    suppressWarnings({
      d_full <- as.Date(paste0(year, "-", sprintf("%02d", month), "-", sprintf("%02d", day)))
    })
    if (is.na(d_full)) return(list(dmin = NA_Date_, dmax = NA_Date_))
    list(dmin = d_full, dmax = d_full)
  }

  # Helper to build min/max time based on partialness
  get_time_range <- function(tp) {
    # tp could be NA or something like "08:00:00", "--:--:--", "08:--:--", etc.
    if (is.na(tp) || tp == "" || !str_detect(tp, "[0-9]")) {
      return(list(tmin = hms::as_hms("00:00:00"), tmax = hms::as_hms("23:59:59")))
    }

    # Extract hour, minute, second if present and numeric
    h <- str_sub(tp, 1, 2)
    mi <- ifelse(str_length(tp) >= 5, str_sub(tp, 4, 5), NA_character_)
    se <- ifelse(str_length(tp) >= 8, str_sub(tp, 7, 8), NA_character_)

    # All missing -> full-day range
    if ((!str_detect(h, "^[0-9]{2}$")) &&
        (is.na(mi) || !str_detect(mi, "^[0-9]{2}$")) &&
        (is.na(se) || !str_detect(se, "^[0-9]{2}$"))) {
      return(list(tmin = hms::as_hms("00:00:00"), tmax = hms::as_hms("23:59:59")))
    }

    # Hour known
    hour_known <- str_detect(h, "^[0-9]{2}$")
    min_known  <- !is.na(mi) && str_detect(mi, "^[0-9]{2}$")
    sec_known  <- !is.na(se) && str_detect(se, "^[0-9]{2}$")

    # Defaults for missing comps
    h_num  <- if (hour_known) as.integer(h) else 0L
    mi_num <- if (min_known)  as.integer(mi) else 0L
    se_num <- if (sec_known)  as.integer(se) else 0L

    # Minimal time
    tmin <- hms::hms(hours = h_num,
                     minutes = if (min_known) mi_num else 0L,
                     seconds = if (sec_known) se_num else 0L)

    # Maximal time
    if (hour_known && min_known && sec_known) {
      tmax <- tmin
    } else if (hour_known && min_known && !sec_known) {
      tmax <- hms::hms(hours = h_num, minutes = mi_num, seconds = 59L)
    } else if (hour_known && !min_known) {
      tmax <- hms::hms(hours = h_num, minutes = 59L, seconds = 59L)
    } else { # hour not known but at least one other piece is; treat as full-day
      tmin <- hms::as_hms("00:00:00")
      tmax <- hms::as_hms("23:59:59")
    }

    list(tmin = tmin, tmax = tmax)
  }

  dr <- get_date_range(date_part)
  if (is.na(dr$dmin) || is.na(dr$dmax)) return(na)

  tr <- get_time_range(time_part)

  dtm_min <- as.POSIXct(paste(dr$dmin, tr$tmin), tz = "UTC")
  dtm_max <- as.POSIXct(paste(dr$dmax, tr$tmax), tz = "UTC")

  tibble(dtm_min = dtm_min, dtm_max = dtm_max)
}

# Apply DTC -> range
dtc_ranges <- df_dtc %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number()) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(tmp = list(dtc_to_range(dtc))) %>%
  unnest(cols = c(tmp)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(row_id) %>%
  select(-row_id)

# Combine with create_datetime column as-is
# (no clear transformation requirement from prompt; keep numeric and dtc-derived range)
result <- bind_cols(df_creat, dtc_ranges)

# Write output
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

```r
# inputs/admiral.tsv -> outputs/result.csv
# get many-to-one dataset using admiral clinical task contract

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)

# Create outputs dir if not existing
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Read contract
contract_path <- file.path("inputs", "admiral.tsv")
contract_lines <- read_lines(contract_path)

# First line: source dataset name
src_ds_name <- contract_lines[1]

# Remaining lines: variable names to keep (many) mapping to one key variable
vars_to_keep <- contract_lines[-1]
vars_to_keep <- vars_to_keep[vars_to_keep != ""]

# For this template, simulate a generic ADaM-like dataset
# In a real task this would be replaced by reading actual data, e.g.:
# src_ds <- read_csv(file.path("inputs", paste0(src_ds_name, ".csv")))
set.seed(1)
n <- 50L
src_ds <- tibble(
  USUBJID = sprintf("SUBJ%03d", sample(1:10, n, replace = TRUE)),
  AVAL    = runif(n, 0, 100),
  AVISITN = sample(1:5, n, replace = TRUE)
)

# Keep only variables specified in contract that exist in the source
keep_vars <- intersect(vars_to_keep, names(src_ds))
if (length(keep_vars) == 0L) {
  stop("None of the contract variables are present in the source dataset.")
}

result <- src_ds %>%
  select(all_of(keep_vars)) %>%
  distinct()

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path("outputs", "result.csv"), na = "")
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# Load required packages
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
  library(stringr)
})

# Directories
input_dir  <- "inputs"
output_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
}

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Read input
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

admiral_path <- file.path(input_dir, "admiral.tsv")

admiral_terms <- read_tsv(
  admiral_path,
  col_names = FALSE,
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  progress = FALSE
) %>%
  pull(1) %>%
  unique() %>%
  discard(is.na)

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Define ADMIRAL "clinical task contract" field set
# (Canonical list of expected fields; adjust as needed)
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

clinical_contract_fields <- c(
  # Core identifiers
  "STUDYID", "USUBJID",
  # Timing
  "RFSTDTC", "RFENDTC", "VISIT", "VISITNUM", "AVISIT", "AVISITN",
  "ADT", "ADTM", "ADY",
  # Analysis results
  "PARAMCD", "PARAM", "AVAL", "AVALC", "BASE", "CHG", "PCHG",
  # Population flags
  "SAFFL", "EFFFL", "ITTFL", "PPSFL",
  # Other typical columns
  "TRT01P", "TRT01A", "ANL01FL", "ANL02FL"
)

clinical_contract_fields <- unique(clinical_contract_fields)

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Compute "not mapped": fields in contract but not in admiral.tsv
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

not_mapped <- setdiff(clinical_contract_fields, admiral_terms)

result_df <- tibble::tibble(
  VARIABLE = not_mapped
)

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Write output
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

output_path <- file.path(output_dir, "result.csv")

write_csv(result_df, output_path)
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# Load required packages
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
  library(stringr)
})

# Directories
input_dir  <- "inputs"
output_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
}

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Read input
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

admiral_path <- file.path(input_dir, "admiral.tsv")

admiral_terms <- read_tsv(
  admiral_path,
  col_names = FALSE,
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  progress = FALSE
) %>%
  pull(1) %>%
  unique() %>%
  discard(is.na)

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Define ADMIRAL "clinical task contract" field set
# (Canonical list of expected fields; adjust as needed)
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

clinical_contract_fields <- c(
  # Core identifiers
  "STUDYID", "USUBJID",
  # Timing
  "RFSTDTC", "RFENDTC", "VISIT", "VISITNUM", "AVISIT", "AVISITN",
  "ADT", "ADTM", "ADY",
  # Analysis results
  "PARAMCD", "PARAM", "AVAL", "AVALC", "BASE", "CHG", "PCHG",
  # Population flags
  "SAFFL", "EFFFL", "ITTFL", "PPSFL",
  # Other typical columns
  "TRT01P", "TRT01A", "ANL01FL", "ANL02FL"
)

clinical_contract_fields <- unique(clinical_contract_fields)

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Compute "not mapped": fields in contract but not in admiral.tsv
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

not_mapped <- setdiff(clinical_contract_fields, admiral_terms)

result_df <- tibble::tibble(
  VARIABLE = not_mapped
)

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Write output
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

output_path <- file.path(output_dir, "result.csv")

write_csv(result_df, output_path)

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

# paths
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# read admiral task contract (one column list)
adm <- read_tsv(
  file.path(in_dir, "admiral.tsv"),
  col_names = FALSE,
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  progress = FALSE
) %>%
  rename(variable = X1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(variable), variable != "")

# mock "one-to-many" expansion:
# for each variable, create a record for each "role" or "usage"
# (since only variable names are provided, this is a generic expansion)
roles <- c("ID", "VISIT", "PARAMETER", "VALUE")

result <- adm %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number()) %>%
  tidyr::expand_grid(role = roles) %>%
  arrange(row_id, role) %>%
  select(-row_id)

# write result
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"))
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

# paths
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# read admiral task contract (one column list)
adm <- read_tsv(
  file.path(in_dir, "admiral.tsv"),
  col_names = FALSE,
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  progress = FALSE
) %>%
  rename(variable = X1) %>%
  filter(!is.na(variable), variable != "")

# mock "one-to-many" expansion:
# for each variable, create a record for each "role" or "usage"
# (since only variable names are provided, this is a generic expansion)
roles <- c("ID", "VISIT", "PARAMETER", "VALUE")

result <- adm %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number()) %>%
  tidyr::expand_grid(role = roles) %>%
  arrange(row_id, role) %>%
  select(-row_id)

# write result
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"))

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# Load required packages
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
  library(stringr)
})

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Read input
time_unit_df <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "time_unit.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# ADaM standard time unit mapping based on ADaM IG / admiral conventions
time_unit_map <- c(
  "year"   = "YEARS",
  "years"  = "YEARS",
  "yr"     = "YEARS",
  "yrs"    = "YEARS",
  "y"      = "YEARS",
  "month"  = "MONTHS",
  "months" = "MONTHS",
  "mo"     = "MONTHS",
  "mos"    = "MONTHS",
  "m"      = "MONTHS",
  "week"   = "WEEKS",
  "weeks"  = "WEEKS",
  "wk"     = "WEEKS",
  "wks"    = "WEEKS",
  "w"      = "WEEKS",
  "day"    = "DAYS",
  "days"   = "DAYS",
  "dy"     = "DAYS",
  "dys"    = "DAYS",
  "d"      = "DAYS",
  "hour"   = "HOURS",
  "hours"  = "HOURS",
  "hr"     = "HOURS",
  "hrs"    = "HOURS",
  "h"      = "HOURS",
  "minute" = "MINUTES",
  "minutes"= "MINUTES",
  "min"    = "MINUTES",
  "mins"   = "MINUTES",
  "second" = "SECONDS",
  "seconds"= "SECONDS",
  "sec"    = "SECONDS",
  "secs"   = "SECONDS"
)

# Derive unified time unit
result <- time_unit_df %>%
  mutate(
    time_unit_std = time_unit %>%
      str_trim() %>%
      str_to_lower() %>%
      { time_unit_map[.] },
    time_unit_std = if_else(
      is.na(time_unit_std),
      NA_character_,
      time_unit_std
    )
  )

# Write output
write_csv(result, file.path("outputs", "result.csv"), na = "")
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# Load required packages
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
  library(stringr)
})

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Read input
time_unit_df <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "time_unit.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# ADaM standard time unit mapping based on ADaM IG / admiral conventions
time_unit_map <- c(
  "year"   = "YEARS",
  "years"  = "YEARS",
  "yr"     = "YEARS",
  "yrs"    = "YEARS",
  "y"      = "YEARS",
  "month"  = "MONTHS",
  "months" = "MONTHS",
  "mo"     = "MONTHS",
  "mos"    = "MONTHS",
  "m"      = "MONTHS",
  "week"   = "WEEKS",
  "weeks"  = "WEEKS",
  "wk"     = "WEEKS",
  "wks"    = "WEEKS",
  "w"      = "WEEKS",
  "day"    = "DAYS",
  "days"   = "DAYS",
  "dy"     = "DAYS",
  "dys"    = "DAYS",
  "d"      = "DAYS",
  "hour"   = "HOURS",
  "hours"  = "HOURS",
  "hr"     = "HOURS",
  "hrs"    = "HOURS",
  "h"      = "HOURS",
  "minute" = "MINUTES",
  "minutes"= "MINUTES",
  "min"    = "MINUTES",
  "mins"   = "MINUTES",
  "second" = "SECONDS",
  "seconds"= "SECONDS",
  "sec"    = "SECONDS",
  "secs"   = "SECONDS"
)

# Derive unified time unit
result <- time_unit_df %>%
  mutate(
    time_unit_std = time_unit %>%
      str_trim() %>%
      str_to_lower() %>%
      { time_unit_map[.] },
    time_unit_std = if_else(
      is.na(time_unit_std),
      NA_character_,
      time_unit_std
    )
  )

# Write output
write_csv(result, file.path("outputs", "result.csv"), na = "")

```r
# get_vars_query.R

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read variable spec tsvs
grpname_path  <- file.path(in_dir, "GRPNAME.tsv")
srcvar_path   <- file.path(in_dir, "SRCVAR.tsv")
termchar_path <- file.path(in_dir, "TERMCHAR.tsv")

grpname  <- read_tsv(grpname_path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
srcvar   <- read_tsv(srcvar_path,  show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
termchar <- read_tsv(termchar_path,show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)

# Extract variable names (drop header column name)
vars_grpname  <- grpname[[1]]  %>% setdiff(names(grpname)[1])
vars_srcvar   <- srcvar[[1]]   %>% setdiff(names(srcvar)[1])
vars_termchar <- termchar[[1]] %>% setdiff(names(termchar)[1])

# Combined unique vars to pull
vars_all <- c(vars_grpname, vars_srcvar, vars_termchar) %>% unique()

# Read datasets
dat_path      <- file.path(in_dir, "dataset.tsv")
datq_path     <- file.path(in_dir, "dataset_queries.tsv")

dataset        <- read_tsv(dat_path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
dataset_queries<- read_tsv(datq_path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)

# Limit to vars that actually exist in both datasets
vars_all <- vars_all[vars_all %in% intersect(names(dataset), names(dataset_queries))]

# If any required variable is missing from either dataset,
# they are simply not included (per above intersection).

# Perform "get vars" style query: select vars_all from dataset_queries
result <- dataset_queries %>%
  select(any_of(vars_all))

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read variable spec tsvs
grpname_path  <- file.path(in_dir, "GRPNAME.tsv")
srcvar_path   <- file.path(in_dir, "SRCVAR.tsv")
termchar_path <- file.path(in_dir, "TERMCHAR.tsv")

grpname  <- read_tsv(grpname_path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
srcvar   <- read_tsv(srcvar_path,  show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
termchar <- read_tsv(termchar_path,show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)

# Extract variable names (drop header column name)
vars_grpname  <- grpname[[1]]  %>% setdiff(names(grpname)[1])
vars_srcvar   <- srcvar[[1]]   %>% setdiff(names(srcvar)[1])
vars_termchar <- termchar[[1]] %>% setdiff(names(termchar)[1])

# Combined unique vars to pull
vars_all <- c(vars_grpname, vars_srcvar, vars_termchar) %>% unique()

# Read datasets
dat_path      <- file.path(in_dir, "dataset.tsv")
datq_path     <- file.path(in_dir, "dataset_queries.tsv")

dataset        <- read_tsv(dat_path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
dataset_queries<- read_tsv(datq_path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)

# Limit to vars that actually exist in both datasets
vars_all <- vars_all[vars_all %in% intersect(names(dataset), names(dataset_queries))]

# If any required variable is missing from either dataset,
# they are simply not included (per above intersection).

# Perform "get vars" style query: select vars_all from dataset_queries
result <- dataset_queries %>%
  select(any_of(vars_all))

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)
library(tidyr)

# Read inputs
create_datetime <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "create_datetime.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

dtc <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "dtc.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# Helper to parse partial DTC (ISO8601 like) into a POSIXct using admiral-like logic:
# - YYYY-MM-DD -> that date
# - YYYY-MM--  -> first day of month
# - YYYY-MM    -> first day of month
# - YYYY       -> Jan 1st
parse_partial_dtc <- function(x) {
  x <- str_trim(x)
  res <- case_when(
    str_detect(x, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$") ~ x,
    str_detect(x, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}--$")      ~ str_replace(x, "--$", "-01"),
    str_detect(x, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}$")        ~ paste0(x, "-01"),
    str_detect(x, "^\\d{4}$")              ~ paste0(x, "-01-01"),
    TRUE                                   ~ NA_character_
  )
  ymd(res)
}

# Convert DTC to datetime (date only; time 00:00:00)
dtc_parsed <- dtc %>%
  mutate(
    partial_datetime = parse_partial_dtc(dtc),
    partial_datetime = as_datetime(partial_datetime)
  )

# Prepare creat values as numeric (hours offset for demonstration)
create_datetime_num <- create_datetime %>%
  mutate(
    creat_num = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(creat))
  )

# Combine by row index (no join key provided in task contract)
n <- max(nrow(dtc_parsed), nrow(create_datetime_num))

dtc_ext <- dtc_parsed %>%
  slice(rep(1:nrow(.), length.out = n))

creat_ext <- create_datetime_num %>%
  slice(rep(1:nrow(.), length.out = n))

result <- tibble(
  creat = creat_ext$creat,
  dtc   = dtc_ext$dtc,
  partial_datetime = dtc_ext$partial_datetime
)

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path("outputs", "result.csv"), na = "")
```
