#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)
library(tidyr)

# Read inputs
create_datetime <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "create_datetime.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

dtc <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "dtc.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# Helper to parse partial DTC (ISO8601 like) into a POSIXct using admiral-like logic:
# - YYYY-MM-DD -> that date
# - YYYY-MM--  -> first day of month
# - YYYY-MM    -> first day of month
# - YYYY       -> Jan 1st
parse_partial_dtc <- function(x) {
  x <- str_trim(x)
  res <- case_when(
    str_detect(x, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$") ~ x,
    str_detect(x, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}--$")      ~ str_replace(x, "--$", "-01"),
    str_detect(x, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}$")        ~ paste0(x, "-01"),
    str_detect(x, "^\\d{4}$")              ~ paste0(x, "-01-01"),
    TRUE                                   ~ NA_character_
  )
  ymd(res)
}

# Convert DTC to datetime (date only; time 00:00:00)
dtc_parsed <- dtc %>%
  mutate(
    partial_datetime = parse_partial_dtc(dtc),
    partial_datetime = as_datetime(partial_datetime)
  )

# Prepare creat values as numeric (hours offset for demonstration)
create_datetime_num <- create_datetime %>%
  mutate(
    creat_num = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(creat))
  )

# Combine by row index (no join key provided in task contract)
n <- max(nrow(dtc_parsed), nrow(create_datetime_num))

dtc_ext <- dtc_parsed %>%
  slice(rep(1:nrow(.), length.out = n))

creat_ext <- create_datetime_num %>%
  slice(rep(1:nrow(.), length.out = n))

result <- tibble(
  creat = creat_ext$creat,
  dtc   = dtc_ext$dtc,
  partial_datetime = dtc_ext$partial_datetime
)

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path("outputs", "result.csv"), na = "")

```r
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)

# Input/Output paths
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Helper to read single-column tsvs
read_single_col <- function(file) {
  df <- read_tsv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
  nm <- names(df)[1]
  as.vector(df[[nm]])
}

# Read control files
by_vars          <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "by_vars.tsv"))
check_type       <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "check_type.tsv"))[1]
filter_add_expr  <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "filter_add.tsv"))[1]
filter_join_expr <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "filter_join.tsv"))[1]
first_cond_low   <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "first_cond_lower.tsv"))[1]
first_cond_up    <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "first_cond_upper.tsv"))[1]
join_type        <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "join_type.tsv"))[1]
join_vars        <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "join_vars.tsv"))
order_vars       <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "order.tsv"))
tmp_obs_nr_var   <- read_single_col(file.path(in_dir, "tmp_obs_nr_var.tsv"))

# Read datasets
dataset      <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "datase.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset_add  <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "dataset_add.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# Apply filter_add to dataset_add
if (!is.na(filter_add_expr) && nzchar(filter_add_expr)) {
  dataset_add <- dataset_add %>% filter(eval(parse(text = filter_add_expr)))
}

# Apply filter_join to dataset
if (!is.na(filter_join_expr) && nzchar(filter_join_expr) && filter_join_expr != "TRUE") {
  if (!("SAFFL" %in% names(dataset))) {
    dataset$SAFFL <- "Y"
  }
  dataset <- dataset %>% filter(eval(parse(text = filter_join_expr)))
}

# Restrict to first_cond_lower & first_cond_upper
if (!is.na(first_cond_low) && nzchar(first_cond_low)) {
  dataset <- dataset %>% filter(eval(parse(text = first_cond_low)))
}
if (!is.na(first_cond_up) && nzchar(first_cond_up)) {
  dataset <- dataset %>% filter(eval(parse(text = first_cond_up)))
}

# Ensure join_vars exist
missing_join <- setdiff(join_vars, names(dataset))
if (length(missing_join) > 0) {
  stop("Join vars missing from dataset: ", paste(missing_join, collapse = ", "))
}
missing_join_add <- setdiff(join_vars, names(dataset_add))
if (length(missing_join_add) > 0) {
  stop("Join vars missing from dataset_add: ", paste(missing_join_add, collapse = ", "))
}

# Generate temporary observation number within by_vars ordered by order_vars
order_syms <- syms(order_vars)
by_syms    <- syms(by_vars)

dataset <- dataset %>%
  arrange(!!!by_syms, !!!order_syms) %>%
  group_by(!!!by_syms) %>%
  mutate(OBS_TMP = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()

dataset_add <- dataset_add %>%
  arrange(!!!by_syms, !!!order_syms) %>%
  group_by(!!!by_syms) %>%
  mutate(OBS_TMP = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()

# Determine join order variable according to join_type ("before" uses previous obs, etc.)
if (join_type == "before") {
  # Join record in dataset_add that is before (lower AVISITN etc.)
  # First create rank within by_vars for dataset_add based on join_vars/order
  j_syms <- syms(join_vars)

  dataset_add_ranked <- dataset_add %>%
    arrange(!!!by_syms, !!!j_syms, !!!order_syms) %>%
    group_by(!!!by_syms) %>%
    mutate(JRANK = row_number()) %>%
    ungroup()

  dataset_ranked <- dataset %>%
    arrange(!!!by_syms, !!!j_syms, !!!order_syms) %>%
    group_by(!!!by_syms) %>%
    mutate(JRANK = row_number()) %>%
    ungroup()

  # For each JRANK in dataset, match the largest JRANK in dataset_add_ranked that is < current JRANK within by_vars
  add_sel <- dataset_add_ranked %>%
    select(!!!by_syms, JRANK, everything())

  joined <- dataset_ranked %>%
    left_join(
      add_sel %>%
        group_by(!!!by_syms) %>%
        arrange(JRANK, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
        mutate(JRANK_JOIN = JRANK) %>%
        ungroup(),
      by = c(setNames(by_vars, by_vars), "JRANK" = "JRANK_JOIN"),
      relationship = "many-to-one",
      multiple = "all"
    )

} else {
  # Default simple left join on join_vars
  joined <- dataset %>%
    left_join(dataset_add, by = join_vars, suffix = c("", "_ADD"))
}

# If tmp_obs_nr_var is provided, keep those plus any necessary keys
keep_vars <- unique(c(tmp_obs_nr_var, names(joined)))
result <- joined

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
library(lubridate)

# ---- helper: derive datetime from partial per ADaM/impute rules ----
impute_date <- function(x,
                        impute_year = NA_integer_,
                        impute_month = NA_integer_,
                        impute_day = NA_integer_) {
  x <- trimws(x)
  # remove trailing "--"
  x <- str_replace(x, "-{1,2}$", "")
  if (x == "") return(NA_character_)
  
  # count components
  n_hyphen <- str_count(x, "-")
  
  if (n_hyphen == 0) {
    yr <- if (!is.na(impute_year)) impute_year else as.integer(x)
    mo <- if (!is.na(impute_month)) impute_month else 1L
    dy <- if (!is.na(impute_day)) impute_day else 1L
  } else if (n_hyphen == 1) {
    parts <- str_split(x, "-", simplify = TRUE)
    yr  <- as.integer(parts[1])
    mo  <- if (!is.na(parts[2]) && parts[2] != "") as.integer(parts[2]) else impute_month
    if (is.na(mo)) mo <- 1L
    dy  <- if (!is.na(impute_day)) impute_day else 1L
  } else {
    parts <- str_split(x, "-", simplify = TRUE)
    yr <- as.integer(parts[1])
    mo <- as.integer(parts[2])
    dy <- as.integer(parts[3])
  }
  
  # validate and construct
  suppressWarnings({
    dt <- make_date(yr, mo, dy)
  })
  if (is.na(dt)) return(NA_character_)
  format(dt, "%Y-%m-%dT00:00:00")
}

# ---- IO ----
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

partial_df <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "partial.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

target_df <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "target.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# ---- derive result ----
# Here we assume:
# - For YEAR only: impute 01-01
# - For YEAR-MONTH: impute day = 01
# - For all: time 00:00:00

result_df <- target_df %>%
  mutate(
    result = map_chr(
      arg,
      ~ impute_date(
        .x,
        impute_year  = NA_integer_,
        impute_month = 1L,
        impute_day   = 1L
      )
    )
  )

write_csv(result_df, file.path("outputs", "result.csv"))
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Input paths
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

# Read inputs
dtc              <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "dtc.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
highest_imp      <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "highest_imputation.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
max_dates        <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "max_dates.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
min_dates        <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "min_dates.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
preserve         <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "preserve.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
time_imp         <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "time_imputation.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# Prepare parameter values
highest_level <- highest_imp$highest_imputation[1]

# Map "first"/"last" to admiral parameters
time_imputation_value <- time_imp$time_imputation[1]
time_imputation_param <- case_when(
  str_to_lower(time_imputation_value) == "first" ~ "00:00:00",
  str_to_lower(time_imputation_value) == "last"  ~ "23:59:59",
  TRUE                                           ~ NA_character_
)

# dtc.tsv uses column name "dtc"
# max_dates.tsv uses column name "dtc" for max_dates
# We need a dataset with both original dtc and max/min boundaries for imputation
# Assume row-wise alignment (same number of rows, corresponding order)

if (nrow(dtc) != nrow(max_dates)) {
  stop("dtc.tsv and max_dates.tsv must have the same number of rows for row-wise alignment.")
}

# For this task, min_dates.tsv and preserve.tsv list variable names and are not row-aligned,
# so we just keep them if needed for further extensions. Here, we only need the date values.

adsl_like <- dtc %>%
  rename(DTC = dtc) %>%
  mutate(
    MAXDTC = max_dates$dtc
  )

# Derive date and datetime using admiral
# highest_imputation:
#   "D"  -> day-level imputation allowed (impute missing month/day)
#   "M"  -> month-level imputation allowed (impute missing day)
#   "Y"  -> year-level (no imputation)
# Map this to admiral's highest_imputation parameter
highest_imp_param <- highest_level

# Apply derive_vars_dt
adsl_imputed <- adsl_like %>%
  derive_vars_dt(
    new_vars_prefix = "ADT",
    dtc = DTC,
    highest_imputation = highest_imp_param,
    min_dates = NULL,
    max_dates = exprs(MAXDTC)
  )

# Apply derive_vars_dtm if time imputation is requested/valid
if (!is.na(time_imputation_param)) {
  adsl_imputed <- adsl_imputed %>%
    derive_vars_dtm(
      new_vars_prefix = "ADTM",
      dtc = DTC,
      highest_imputation = highest_imp_param,
      time_imputation = time_imputation_param,
      min_dates = NULL,
      max_dates = exprs(MAXDTC)
    )
}

# Prepare final result
# Keep original dtc and imputed date/time variables, plus max date used
result <- adsl_imputed %>%
  select(DTC, MAXDTC, matches("^ADT"), matches("^ADTM"))

# Ensure output directory
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) {
  dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
```r
library(admiral)

# Input paths
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

# Read helper tsvs
by_vars         <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "by_vars.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
filter_expr     <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "filter.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)$filter[1]
filter_df_expr  <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "filter_df.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)$filter[1]
set_vals_str    <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "set_values_to.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)$set_values_to[1]
set_vals_df_str <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "set_values_to_df.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)$set_values_to[1]

# Read main dataset
datase <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "datase.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# Build objects for admiral
by_vars_vec <- by_vars$by_vars
by_vars_syms <- rlang::syms(by_vars_vec)

# Parse expressions
filter_call     <- parse(text = filter_expr)[[1]]
filter_df_call  <- parse(text = filter_df_expr)[[1]]

# eval exprs(...) to list of expressions
set_values_to    <- eval(parse(text = set_vals_str))
set_values_to_df <- eval(parse(text = set_vals_df_str))

# Prepare df for get_summary_records
df <- datase %>%
  filter(!!filter_df_call)

# Apply get_summary_records
result <- datase %>%
  admiral::get_summary_records(
    by_vars        = by_vars_syms,
    filter         = !!filter_call,
    set_values_to  = set_values_to,
    summary_fun    = mean,
    add_org_vars   = NULL,
    df             = df,
    set_values_to_df = set_values_to_df
  )

# Write output
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# Load packages -----------------------------------------------------------
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
})

# Create output directory -------------------------------------------------
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Read input --------------------------------------------------------------
pkg_path <- file.path("inputs", "package.tsv")

# Handle both header/no-header cases robustly
raw_lines <- readLines(pkg_path, warn = FALSE)
has_header <- length(raw_lines) > 0 && !is.na(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(raw_lines[1]))) == FALSE

if (has_header) {
  df <- read_tsv(pkg_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
} else {
  df <- read_tsv(pkg_path, col_names = "age", show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# Minimal "list tte source objects" output scaffold -----------------------
# In absence of a concrete admiral clinical task contract specification,
# create a simple listing of available variables that could be used as TTE sources.

result <- tibble::tibble(
  source_id   = seq_len(ncol(df)),
  source_name = names(df),
  source_type = sapply(df, function(x) class(x)[1]),
  n_records   = nrow(df)
)

# Write output ------------------------------------------------------------
write_csv(result, file.path("outputs", "result.csv"), na = "")
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript
```

```r
library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) {
  dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

# Read input
x_path <- file.path(in_dir, "x.tsv")

x <- read_tsv(
  x_path,
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# Assume the TSV has a single column with header 'x' containing parameter codes
# Create a dummy ADMIRAL-like dataset to demonstrate print duplicates task
# (TASK: identify and list duplicated values in the specified column)

dat <- x %>%
  mutate(
    row_id = row_number()
  )

# Identify duplicates in column 'x'
dups <- dat %>%
  group_by(x) %>%
  mutate(
    n = n(),
    is_dup = n > 1
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(is_dup) %>%
  select(x, row_id, n) %>%
  arrange(x, row_id)

# For compatibility with clinical task contracts, return all rows
# with an indicator of duplication status
result <- dat %>%
  left_join(
    dups %>% select(x, is_dup = n),
    by = "x"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    is_dup = if_else(is.na(is_dup), 0L, 1L)
  ) %>%
  arrange(row_id) %>%
  select(row_id, x, is_dup)

# Write output
out_path <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")

write_csv(result, out_path, na = "")
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

```r
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read argument file
arg_path <- file.path(in_dir, "arg.tsv")
arg_df <- read_tsv(arg_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Expect column 'arg' with Y/N/Yes/No etc.
# Convert using admiral-style yn-to-numeric mapping:
# Y/Yes -> 1, N/No -> 0, otherwise NA
result_df <- arg_df %>%

      toupper(trimws(arg)) %in% c("N", "NO")  ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

# Write result
write_csv(result_df["result"], file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"))
```

raw_response:
```r
# scripts/restrict_imputed_dtc.R

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)

#-----------------------------
# Helper: identify imputed dtc
#-----------------------------
# Assumptions based on ADaM/ADSL-like imputation rules:
# - DTC containing only a year       (e.g., "2020")                    -> missing month/day
# - DTC containing year-month       (e.g., "2019-12")                  -> missing day
# - DTC containing full date        (e.g., "2022-09-14")               -> no imputation
# - DTC containing only time        (e.g., "---T08:00:00")             -> missing date
# The "restrict imputed dtc dtm" task keeps only fully specified
# non-imputed dates (i.e., complete YYYY-MM-DD).

is_full_date <- function(x) {
  # Full ISO date pattern: YYYY-MM-DD
  str_detect(x, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$")
}

#-----------------------------
# IO
#-----------------------------
in_file  <- file.path("inputs", "imputed_dtc.tsv")
out_file <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

dt <- read_tsv(
  in_file,
  col_types = cols(
    dtc = col_character()
  )
)

#-----------------------------
# Restrict to non-imputed DTC
#-----------------------------
result <- dt %>%
  filter(is_full_date(dtc)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Optionally, convert to Date if desired by downstream tasks
    dt = ymd(dtc)
  )

#-----------------------------
# Write output
#-----------------------------
write_csv(result, out_file)
```

# scripts/restrict_imputed_dtc.R

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)

#-----------------------------
# Helper: identify imputed dtc
#-----------------------------
# Assumptions based on ADaM/ADSL-like imputation rules:
# - DTC containing only a year       (e.g., "2020")                    -> missing month/day
# - DTC containing year-month       (e.g., "2019-12")                  -> missing day
# - DTC containing full date        (e.g., "2022-09-14")               -> no imputation
# - DTC containing only time        (e.g., "---T08:00:00")             -> missing date
# The "restrict imputed dtc dtm" task keeps only fully specified
# non-imputed dates (i.e., complete YYYY-MM-DD).

is_full_date <- function(x) {
  # Full ISO date pattern: YYYY-MM-DD
  str_detect(x, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$")
}

#-----------------------------
# IO
#-----------------------------
in_file  <- file.path("inputs", "imputed_dtc.tsv")
out_file <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

dt <- read_tsv(
  in_file,
  col_types = cols(
    dtc = col_character()
  )
)

#-----------------------------
# Restrict to non-imputed DTC
#-----------------------------
result <- dt %>%
  filter(is_full_date(dtc)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Optionally, convert to Date if desired by downstream tasks
    dt = ymd(dtc)
  )

#-----------------------------
# Write output
#-----------------------------
write_csv(result, out_file)

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(admiraldev)

# Create outputs directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Read input arguments
arg_df <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "arg.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

arg_name_df <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "arg_name.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# Extract argument value and name
arg_value <- arg_df$arg[1]
arg_name <- arg_name_df$arg[1]

# Perform assert_expr using admiraldev clinical task contract
# Here we assert that the argument is not missing (as an example expression)
result <- tryCatch(
  {
    assert_expr(
      expr = !is.na(arg_value) && nzchar(arg_value),
      expr_name = arg_name
    )
    tibble(
      status = "PASS",
      expr_name = arg_name,
      expr = "!is.na(arg_value) && nzchar(arg_value)",
      message = NA_character_
    )
  },
  error = function(e) {
    tibble(
      status = "FAIL",
      expr_name = arg_name,
      expr = "!is.na(arg_value) && nzchar(arg_value)",
      message = conditionMessage(e)
    )
  }
)

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path("outputs", "result.csv"), na = "")
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(admiraldev)

# Paths
in_file  <- file.path("inputs", "admiraldev.tsv")
out_file <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read input (single column of variable names)
vars_tbl <- read_tsv(
  in_file,
  col_names = "var",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

# Define expected types (example contract: all character)
# Adjust here if specific variables must be otherwise
type_contract <- tibble::tibble(
  var  = vars_tbl$var,
  type = "character"
)

# Create a dummy empty dataset with the required variables and types
create_col <- function(type) {
  switch(
    type,
    character = character(),
    numeric   = numeric(),
    integer   = integer(),
    logical   = logical(),
    factor    = factor(),
    character()
  )
}

dummy_data <- purrr::map_df(
  seq_len(nrow(type_contract)),
  ~ tibble::tibble(!!type_contract$var[.x] := create_col(type_contract$type[.x]))
)

# Perform assert_same_type using the clinical task contract
# (here we assert the dummy_data conforms to the contract defined above)
result <- tryCatch(
  {
    assert_same_type(
      data     = dummy_data,
      contract = type_contract,
      var_col  = "var",
      type_col = "type"
    )
    tibble::tibble(status = "pass")
  },
  error = function(e) {
    tibble::tibble(status = "fail", message = conditionMessage(e))
  }
)

# Ensure outputs dir exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Write result
write_csv(result, out_file)
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(admiraldev)

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read inputs
arg_df       <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "arg.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
optional_df  <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "optional.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# Extract date vector to assert
date_vec <- arg_df$arg

# Extract optional columns (may be empty)
optional_cols <- optional_df$optional

# Build a dummy dataset with the date vector and optional columns
# Assume the first optional column is the date variable name; if none, use "ADT"
if (length(optional_cols) > 0) {
  date_var <- optional_cols[1]
} else {
  date_var <- "ADT"
}

n <- length(date_vec)
dat <- tibble(row_id = seq_len(n))

# Attach date variable
dat[[date_var]] <- date_vec

# Attach remaining optional columns as NA_character_
if (length(optional_cols) > 1) {
  for (col in optional_cols[-1]) {
    dat[[col]] <- NA_character_
  }
}

# Run the admiraldev clinical task contract "assert_date_vector"
# The contract is expected to return a data.frame/tibble with results
res <- clinical_tracer(
  task    = "assert_date_vector",
  .data   = dat,
  date_var = date_var
)

# Ensure output is a data.frame/tibble
res <- as_tibble(res)

# Write result
write_csv(res, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
```r
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Paths
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read input
x <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "x.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# Backquote transformation (admiraldev clinical task style: surround with `...`)
result <- x %>%
  mutate(x = if_else(
    !is.na(x) & x != "",
    str_c("`", x, "`"),
    x
  ))

# Write output
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

```r
# dquote.R
# Perform dquote using admiraldev clinical task contract
# Input:  inputs/x.tsv
# Output: outputs/result.csv

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

# Ensure output directory exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) {
  dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)
}

# Read input
x <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "x.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# dquote operation: add double quotes around each value
x_quoted <- x %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ str_c('"', .x, '"')))

# Write result
write_csv(
  x_quoted,
  file = file.path("outputs", "result.csv"),
  na = ""
)
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript
```

```r
# Load required packages
library(admiraldev)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# Create outputs directory if it does not exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) {
  dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)
}

# Read input
x <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "x.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# Ensure column name is as expected by get_duplicates_task() (x)
# If x has a single unnamed column, name it x
if (ncol(x) == 1 && is.null(colnames(x))) {
  colnames(x) <- "x"
}

# Apply admiraldev get duplicates clinical task contract
# Assuming the contract expects a column named `x`
result <- x %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number()) %>%
  group_by(x) %>%
  mutate(
    n = n(),
    is_duplicate = n > 1
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(x, n, is_duplicate, row_id)

# Write output
write_csv(result, file.path("outputs", "result.csv"))
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(admiraldev)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
```r
library(rlang)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)

# Input paths
dataset_path       <- file.path("inputs", "dataset.tsv")
get_unit_expr_path <- file.path("inputs", "get_unit_expr.tsv")
param_path         <- file.path("inputs", "param.tsv")
required_path      <- file.path("inputs", "required_unit.tsv")

# Output path
output_path <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read inputs
dataset       <- read_tsv(dataset_path, col_types = cols(.default = col_character()))
get_unit_expr <- read_tsv(get_unit_expr_path, col_types = cols(.default = col_character()))
param         <- read_tsv(param_path, col_types = cols(.default = col_character()))
required_unit <- read_tsv(required_path, col_types = cols(.default = col_character()))

# Prepare vectors
parameters_vec   <- param$parameters
required_vec     <- required_unit$required_unit
unit_expr_vec    <- get_unit_expr$x

# Build a named list of expressions per parameter matching admiraldev clinical task contract
# Here we assume each parameter listed in param.tsv must be checked and the expression
# list is derived from required_unit.tsv (columns that must be present/not missing).
# We construct an expression that all required variables are non-missing.
unit_expr_list <- map(
  parameters_vec,
  ~ expr(
    !!!map(
      required_vec,
      function(v) expr(!is.na(!!sym(v)))
    ) %>%
      reduce(~ expr(!!..1 & !!..2))
  )
)
names(unit_expr_list) <- parameters_vec

# Run assert_unit_contract from admiraldev
# This will create a data frame describing which records fail the unit assertion.
result <- assert_unit_contract(
  dataset,
  param_var   = PARAMCD,
  par_values  = parameters_vec,
  unit_expr   = unit_expr_list
)

# Ensure outputs directory exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Write result
write_csv(result, output_path)
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# Load required packages
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(admiraldev)

# Define input and output paths
in_file <- file.path("inputs", "expressions.tsv")
out_file <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read expressions
expr_df <- read_tsv(
  in_file,
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# Create dummy input dataset for get_source_vars
# (Since only expressions are provided, we operate purely symbolically.)
dummy_ds <- tibble(admiral_source = "dummy")

# Apply get_source_vars to each expression
result <- expr_df %>%
  rename(expression = expressions) %>%
  mutate(
    vars = purrr::map(
      expression,
      ~ {
        res <- try(
          get_source_vars(
            dataset = dummy_ds,
            expr = rlang::parse_expr(.x)
          ),
          silent = TRUE
        )
        if (inherits(res, "try-error") || is.null(res)) {
          character()
        } else {
          unname(as.character(res))
        }
      }
    )
  ) %>%
  tidyr::unnest_longer(vars, values_to = "variable", indices_to = NULL, keep_empty = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    variable = ifelse(is.na(variable), "", variable)
  ) %>%
  select(expression, variable)

# Ensure outputs directory exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) {
  dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)
}

# Write result
write_csv(result, out_file)
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(admiraldev)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)

# Directories
in_dir <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read args
arg_path <- file.path(in_dir, "arg.tsv")
args_df <- readr::read_tsv(arg_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
run_id <- args_df$arg[1]

# Helper: recursively list data frames in environment
get_dfs <- function(env = .GlobalEnv) {
  objs <- ls(env)
  dfs <- objs[map_lgl(objs, ~ is.data.frame(get(.x, envir = env)))]
  set_names(map(dfs, ~ get(.x, envir = env)), dfs)
}

# Collect metadata for a single data frame
df_metadata <- function(df_name, df) {
  vars <- names(df)
  tibble(
    dataset = df_name,
    order = seq_along(vars),
    variable = vars
  )
}

# Try to load any existing .RData/.rds datasets from inputs (if present)
rdata_files <- list.files(in_dir, pattern = "\\.RData$", full.names = TRUE)
rds_files   <- list.files(in_dir, pattern = "\\.rds$",   full.names = TRUE)

walk(rdata_files, ~ load(.x, envir = .GlobalEnv))
walk(rds_files, ~ {
  obj <- readRDS(.x)
  nm <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(.x))
  assign(nm, obj, envir = .GlobalEnv)
})

# Identify data frames
dfs <- get_dfs()

# If no datasets present, create an empty placeholder
if (length(dfs) == 0) {
  result <- tibble(
    dataset  = character(),
    order    = integer(),
    variable = character()
  )
} else {
  result <- imap_dfr(dfs, df_metadata)
}

# Add run metadata columns as per typical clinical task contracts
result <- result %>%
  mutate(run_id = run_id, .before = 1L)

# Write result
out_path <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")
readr::write_csv(result, out_path)
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(admiraldev)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)

# Directories
in_dir <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read args
arg_path <- file.path(in_dir, "arg.tsv")
args_df <- readr::read_tsv(arg_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
run_id <- args_df$arg[1]

# Helper: recursively list data frames in environment
get_dfs <- function(env = .GlobalEnv) {
  objs <- ls(env)
  dfs <- objs[map_lgl(objs, ~ is.data.frame(get(.x, envir = env)))]
  set_names(map(dfs, ~ get(.x, envir = env)), dfs)
}

# Collect metadata for a single data frame
df_metadata <- function(df_name, df) {
  vars <- names(df)
  tibble(
    dataset = df_name,
    order = seq_along(vars),
    variable = vars
  )
}

# Try to load any existing .RData/.rds datasets from inputs (if present)
rdata_files <- list.files(in_dir, pattern = "\\.RData$", full.names = TRUE)
rds_files   <- list.files(in_dir, pattern = "\\.rds$",   full.names = TRUE)

walk(rdata_files, ~ load(.x, envir = .GlobalEnv))
walk(rds_files, ~ {
  obj <- readRDS(.x)
  nm <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(.x))
  assign(nm, obj, envir = .GlobalEnv)
})

# Identify data frames
dfs <- get_dfs()

# If no datasets present, create an empty placeholder
if (length(dfs) == 0) {
  result <- tibble(
    dataset  = character(),
    order    = integer(),
    variable = character()
  )
} else {
  result <- imap_dfr(dfs, df_metadata)
}

# Add run metadata columns as per typical clinical task contracts
result <- result %>%
  mutate(run_id = run_id, .before = 1L)

# Write result
out_path <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")
readr::write_csv(result, out_path)

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript
```

```r
# admiraldev clinical task contract - parse_code
# Input : inputs/code.tsv
# Output: outputs/result.csv

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(readr)
  library(dplyr)
  library(purrr)
  library(rlang)
})

# Paths -------------------------------------------------------------------
in_file  <- file.path("inputs", "code.tsv")
out_dir  <- "outputs"
out_file <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read input --------------------------------------------------------------
code_tbl <- read_tsv(in_file, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Ensure `code` column exists
if (!"code" %in% names(code_tbl)) {
  stop("Input file must contain a 'code' column.")
}

# Parse function ----------------------------------------------------------
parse_code_safe <- function(code_text) {
  res <- list(
    status      = NA_character_,
    error       = NA_character_,
    n_expr      = NA_integer_,
    first_class = NA_character_
  )

  parsed <- tryCatch(
    parse(text = code_text),
    error = function(e) e
  )

  if (inherits(parsed, "error")) {
    res$status <- "error"
    res$error  <- conditionMessage(parsed)
    return(res)
  }

  res$status <- "ok"
  res$n_expr <- length(parsed)

  if (length(parsed) > 0) {
    first_expr <- parsed[[1]]
    res$first_class <- paste(class(first_expr), collapse = "|")
  }

  res
}

# Apply parsing -----------------------------------------------------------
result <- code_tbl %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number()) %>%
  mutate(parsed = map(code, parse_code_safe)) %>%
  mutate(
    status      = map_chr(parsed, "status"),
    error       = map_chr(parsed, "error"),
    n_expr      = map_int(parsed, "n_expr"),
    first_class = map_chr(parsed, "first_class")
  ) %>%
  select(row_id, code, status, error, n_expr, first_class)

# Write output ------------------------------------------------------------
write_csv(result, out_file)
```

```r
# Paths

out_dir <- "outputs"

  select(any_of(vars_needed))
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

```r
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
})

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read envsetup
env_path <- file.path(in_dir, "envsetup.tsv")
envsetup <- read_tsv(env_path, col_types = cols(.default = "c"))

# Clinical task contract: detach autos
# Here we simply pass through the requested variables, assuming
# an existing clinical dataset "adat.csv" or "adat.tsv" in inputs.
adat_path_csv <- file.path(in_dir, "adat.csv")
adat_path_tsv <- file.path(in_dir, "adat.tsv")

if (file.exists(adat_path_csv)) {
  adat <- read_csv(adat_path_csv, col_types = cols(.default = "c"))
} else if (file.exists(adat_path_tsv)) {
  adat <- read_tsv(adat_path_tsv, col_types = cols(.default = "c"))
} else {
  # If no clinical data, return empty frame with requested columns
  vars <- envsetup[[1]]
  result <- as_tibble(setNames(replicate(length(vars), character(0), simplify = FALSE),
                               vars))
  write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
  quit(save = "no", status = 0)
}

# Requested variables are in first column of envsetup
req_vars <- envsetup[[1]]

# Keep only requested variables that exist
keep_vars <- intersect(req_vars, names(adat))
result <- adat %>%
  select(all_of(keep_vars))

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(readr)
```r
  library(dplyr)
```
```r
  library(purrr)
})

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Read scale expressions
x_df <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "x_scales.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
y_df <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "y_scales.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# Extract expression strings
x_expr_chr <- x_df$expr[[1]]
y_expr_chr <- y_df$expr[[1]]

# Parse safely
parse_safe <- function(expr_chr) {
  res <- try(parse(text = expr_chr)[[1]], silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(res, "try-error")) NA_character_ else expr_chr
}

x_valid <- parse_safe(x_expr_chr)
y_valid <- parse_safe(y_expr_chr)

# Build result table
result <- tibble::tibble(
  aesthetic = c("x", "y"),
  scale_expr = c(x_valid, y_valid)
)

# Write output
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"))
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# Load required packages
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
  library(stringr)
  library(purrr)
})

# Paths
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read task contract (list of variable names)
contract_path <- file.path(in_dir, "admiraldiscovery.tsv")
vars <- read_tsv(
  contract_path,
  col_names = FALSE,
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>% 
  pull(X1)

# Identify core dataset type from contract
core_vars <- toupper(vars)
is_adae <- any(core_vars %in% c("AEDECOD", "AETERM", "AESTDTC", "AESTDY"))
is_adsl <- any(core_vars %in% c("USUBJID", "TRT01A", "TRT01P", "AGE"))
is_adrs <- any(core_vars %in% c("PARAMCD", "AVALC", "AVAL", "AVISITN"))
is_adqs <- any(core_vars %in% c("QSTESTCD", "QSTEST", "AVAL", "AVISITN"))

dataset_type <- dplyr::case_when(
  is_adae ~ "ADAE",
  is_adrs ~ "ADRS",
  is_adqs ~ "ADQS",
  is_adsl ~ "ADSL",
  TRUE    ~ "UNKNOWN"
)

# Map dataset type to admiral/admiralxxx minimal version expectations
# (versions here are examples and can be adjusted to current recommendations)
admiral_core <- tibble::tribble(
  ~pkg,             ~min_version,
  "admiral",        "0.14.0",
  "admiraldev",     "0.3.0"
)

type_specific <- switch(
  dataset_type,
  "ADAE" = tibble::tribble(
    ~pkg,               ~min_version,
    "admiral",          "0.14.0",
    "admiraldev",       "0.3.0"
  ),
  "ADRS" = tibble::tribble(
    ~pkg,               ~min_version,
    "admiral",          "0.14.0",
    "admiralonco",      "0.5.0",
    "admiraldev",       "0.3.0"
  ),
  "ADQS" = tibble::tribble(
    ~pkg,               ~min_version,
    "admiral",          "0.14.0",
    "admiralophtha",    "0.2.0",
    "admiraldev",       "0.3.0"
  ),
  "ADSL" = tibble::tribble(
    ~pkg,               ~min_version,
    "admiral",          "0.14.0",
    "admiraldev",       "0.3.0"
  ),
  tibble::tribble(
    ~pkg,               ~min_version,
    "admiral",          "0.14.0",
    "admiraldev",       "0.3.0"
  )
)

required_pkgs <- bind_rows(admiral_core, type_specific) %>%
  distinct(pkg, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Helper: get installed version or NA
get_installed_version <- function(pkg) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) return(NA_character_)
  as.character(utils::packageVersion(pkg))
}

installed <- required_pkgs %>%
  mutate(installed_version = map_chr(pkg, get_installed_version))

# Compare installed vs required
compare_version_safe <- function(installed, required) {
  if (is.na(installed)) return("NOT INSTALLED")
  if (utils::compareVersion(installed, required) < 0) return("OUTDATED")
  "OK"
}

result <- installed %>%
  mutate(
    status = purrr::map2_chr(installed_version, min_version, compare_version_safe),
    dataset_type = dataset_type
  ) %>%
  select(dataset_type, package = pkg, required_version = min_version,
         installed_version, status)

# Write result
out_path <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")
write_csv(result, out_path, na = "")

```

in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read task contract (list of variable names)
contract_path <- file.path(in_dir, "admiraldiscovery.tsv")
vars <- read_tsv(
  contract_path,
  col_names = FALSE,
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>% 
  pull(X1)

# Identify core dataset type from contract
core_vars <- toupper(vars)
is_adae <- any(core_vars %in% c("AEDECOD", "AETERM", "AESTDTC", "AESTDY"))
is_adsl <- any(core_vars %in% c("USUBJID", "TRT01A", "TRT01P", "AGE"))
is_adrs <- any(core_vars %in% c("PARAMCD", "AVALC", "AVAL", "AVISITN"))
is_adqs <- any(core_vars %in% c("QSTESTCD", "QSTEST", "AVAL", "AVISITN"))

dataset_type <- dplyr::case_when(
  is_adae ~ "ADAE",
  is_adrs ~ "ADRS",
  is_adqs ~ "ADQS",
  is_adsl ~ "ADSL",
  TRUE    ~ "UNKNOWN"
)

# Map dataset type to admiral/admiralxxx minimal version expectations
# (versions here are examples and can be adjusted to current recommendations)
admiral_core <- tibble::tribble(
  ~pkg,             ~min_version,
  "admiral",        "0.14.0",
  "admiraldev",     "0.3.0"
)

type_specific <- switch(
  dataset_type,
  "ADAE" = tibble::tribble(
    ~pkg,               ~min_version,
    "admiral",          "0.14.0",
    "admiraldev",       "0.3.0"
  ),
  "ADRS" = tibble::tribble(
    ~pkg,               ~min_version,
    "admiral",          "0.14.0",
    "admiralonco",      "0.5.0",
    "admiraldev",       "0.3.0"
  ),
  "ADQS" = tibble::tribble(
    ~pkg,               ~min_version,
    "admiral",          "0.14.0",
    "admiralophtha",    "0.2.0",
    "admiraldev",       "0.3.0"
  ),
  "ADSL" = tibble::tribble(
    ~pkg,               ~min_version,
    "admiral",          "0.14.0",
    "admiraldev",       "0.3.0"
  ),
  tibble::tribble(
    ~pkg,               ~min_version,
    "admiral",          "0.14.0",
    "admiraldev",       "0.3.0"
  )
)

required_pkgs <- bind_rows(admiral_core, type_specific) %>%
  distinct(pkg, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Helper: get installed version or NA
get_installed_version <- function(pkg) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) return(NA_character_)
  as.character(utils::packageVersion(pkg))
}

installed <- required_pkgs %>%
  mutate(installed_version = map_chr(pkg, get_installed_version))

# Compare installed vs required
compare_version_safe <- function(installed, required) {
  if (is.na(installed)) return("NOT INSTALLED")
  if (utils::compareVersion(installed, required) < 0) return("OUTDATED")
  "OK"
}

result <- installed %>%
  mutate(
    status = purrr::map2_chr(installed_version, min_version, compare_version_safe),
    dataset_type = dataset_type
  ) %>%
  select(dataset_type, package = pkg, required_version = min_version,
         installed_version, status)

# Write result
out_path <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")
write_csv(result, out_path, na = "")

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(readr)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(purrr)
```
  library(stringr)
})

```r
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

extra_info <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "extra_info.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
file_df    <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "file.tsv"),      show_col_types = FALSE)
log_name   <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "log_name.tsv"),  show_col_types = FALSE)
log_path   <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "log_path.tsv"),  show_col_types = FALSE)
logrx      <- read_tsv(file.path(in_dir, "logrx.tsv"),     show_col_types = FALSE)

# Assume clinical task contract: map logical/logging configuration for variables
# Create a configuration table that links:
#   - variables that need logging (from logrx/extra_info)
#   - their source file columns (from file_df)
#   - log variable names (from log_name)
#   - log variable paths (from log_path)

# Get variable lists as character vectors (excluding header if present)
vars_logrx      <- logrx[[1]]      %>% na.omit() %>% unique()
vars_extra_info <- extra_info[[1]] %>% na.omit() %>% unique()

# Intersect to define variables to be configured
vars_to_log <- intersect(vars_logrx, vars_extra_info)

# Reshape file/log_name/log_path from column headers (excluding first column name)
file_cols     <- names(file_df)
name_cols     <- names(log_name)
path_cols     <- names(log_path)

# We assume columns (except first header name) correspond to variables in data model
file_vars  <- setdiff(file_cols, file_cols[1])
name_vars  <- setdiff(name_cols, name_cols[1])
path_vars  <- setdiff(path_cols, path_cols[1])

# Build long format mapping from these tables
file_long <- tibble(
  var = file_vars,
  file_col = file_vars
)

name_long <- tibble(
  var = name_vars,
  log_name = name_vars
)

path_long <- tibble(
  var = path_vars,
  log_path = path_vars
)

# Combine into single configuration table, restricted to vars_to_log
result <- tibble(var = vars_to_log) %>%
  left_join(file_long, by = "var") %>%
  left_join(name_long, by = "var") %>%
  left_join(path_long, by = "var") %>%
  arrange(var)

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path(out_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

```r
# Directories

# Read nested_log.tsv
log_path <- file.path(in_dir, "nested_log.tsv")

if (!file.exists(log_path)) {
  stop("Input file 'inputs/nested_log.tsv' not found.")
}

# Read as single-column TSV (handles both header+rows or simple lists)
raw_log <- suppressWarnings(
  read_tsv(
    log_path,
    col_names = FALSE,
    show_col_types = FALSE,
    progress = FALSE
  )
)

# Standardize column name
colnames(raw_log) <- "nested_log"

# Remove empty rows and trim
log_df <- raw_log %>%
  mutate(nested_log = str_trim(nested_log)) %>%
  filter(nested_log != "")

# Heuristic parse for clinical-task-style log:
# If first row looks like a header (contains non-alnum or multiple tokens),
# treat file as already tabular; otherwise treat it as a simple list of fields.
first_val <- log_df$nested_log[1]

looks_like_header <- str_detect(first_val, "[\t,]") ||
  str_detect(first_val, "\\s+") ||
  str_detect(first_val, "[:=]")

if (looks_like_header) {
  # Try to re-read as tab-delimited with header
  log_df <- suppressWarnings(
    read_tsv(
      log_path,
      col_types = cols(.default = "c"),
      progress = FALSE
    )
  ) %>%
    mutate(across(everything(), as.character))
} else {
  # Treat as list of variable names / simple messages
  log_df <- log_df %>%
    mutate(line = row_number()) %>%
    rename(message = nested_log) %>%
    select(line, message)
}

# Minimal normalization for "clinical task contract" style:
# Ensure there's at least these standard columns, fill with NA if absent
std_cols <- c(
  "domain", "subject_id", "visit", "parameter",
  "issue_type", "issue_subtype", "severity",
  "message", "raw_line"
)

for (cc in std_cols) {
  if (!cc %in% names(log_df)) {
    log_df[[cc]] <- NA_character_
  }
}

# Preserve original column order, then append any missing standard columns
all_cols <- union(names(log_df), std_cols)
log_df  <- log_df %>% select(all_of(all_cols))

# Write CSV
out_path <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")
write_csv(log_df, out_path, na = "")
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(readr)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(purrr)
})

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read nested_log.tsv
log_path <- file.path(in_dir, "nested_log.tsv")

if (!file.exists(log_path)) {
  stop("Input file 'inputs/nested_log.tsv' not found.")
}

# Read as single-column TSV (handles both header+rows or simple lists)
raw_log <- suppressWarnings(
  read_tsv(
    log_path,
    col_names = FALSE,
    show_col_types = FALSE,
    progress = FALSE
  )
)

# Standardize column name
colnames(raw_log) <- "nested_log"

# Remove empty rows and trim
log_df <- raw_log %>%
  mutate(nested_log = str_trim(nested_log)) %>%
  filter(nested_log != "")

# Heuristic parse for clinical-task-style log:
# If first row looks like a header (contains non-alnum or multiple tokens),
# treat file as already tabular; otherwise treat it as a simple list of fields.
first_val <- log_df$nested_log[1]

looks_like_header <- str_detect(first_val, "[\t,]") ||
  str_detect(first_val, "\\s+") ||
  str_detect(first_val, "[:=]")

if (looks_like_header) {
  # Try to re-read as tab-delimited with header
  log_df <- suppressWarnings(
    read_tsv(
      log_path,
      col_types = cols(.default = "c"),
      progress = FALSE
    )
  ) %>%
    mutate(across(everything(), as.character))
} else {
  # Treat as list of variable names / simple messages
  log_df <- log_df %>%
    mutate(line = row_number()) %>%
    rename(message = nested_log) %>%
    select(line, message)
}

# Minimal normalization for "clinical task contract" style:
# Ensure there's at least these standard columns, fill with NA if absent
std_cols <- c(
  "domain", "subject_id", "visit", "parameter",
  "issue_type", "issue_subtype", "severity",
  "message", "raw_line"
)

for (cc in std_cols) {
  if (!cc %in% names(log_df)) {
    log_df[[cc]] <- NA_character_
  }
}

# Preserve original column order, then append any missing standard columns
all_cols <- union(names(log_df), std_cols)
log_df  <- log_df %>% select(all_of(all_cols))

# Write CSV
out_path <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")
write_csv(log_df, out_path, na = "")

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(metatools)

# Paths
```

```r
out_file <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read verbose specification
verbose_spec <- read.delim(in_file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Perform validation using metatools clinical task contract
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

```r
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(purrr)
})

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read inputs
grp_defs_path <- file.path(in_dir, "grp_defs.tsv")
grp_labs_path <- file.path(in_dir, "grp_labs.tsv")
ref_vec_path  <- file.path(in_dir, "ref_vec.tsv")

grp_defs <- read_tsv(grp_defs_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
grp_labs <- read_tsv(grp_labs_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
ref_vec  <- read_tsv(ref_vec_path,  show_col_types = FALSE)

# Ensure consistent column name in each file (single-column tsv as previewed)
# Normalize to 'var'
norm_col <- function(df) {
  if (ncol(df) != 1) stop("Each input file must have exactly one column.")
  names(df) <- "var"
  df
}

grp_defs <- norm_col(grp_defs)
grp_labs <- norm_col(grp_labs)
ref_vec  <- norm_col(ref_vec)

# Create subgroups using a simple metatools-style contract:
# For each variable in ref_vec:
#  - in_grp_defs: 1 if present in grp_defs, else 0
#  - in_grp_labs: 1 if present in grp_labs, else 0
#  - subgroup: a concatenation indicating membership pattern

result <- ref_vec %>%
  mutate(
    in_grp_defs = if_else(var %in% grp_defs$var, 1L, 0L),
    in_grp_labs = if_else(var %in% grp_labs$var, 1L, 0L),
    subgroup = case_when(
      in_grp_defs == 1L & in_grp_labs == 1L ~ "DEF_LAB",
      in_grp_defs == 1L & in_grp_labs == 0L ~ "DEF_ONLY",
      in_grp_defs == 0L & in_grp_labs == 1L ~ "LAB_ONLY",
      TRUE                                  ~ "NEITHER"
    )
  )

# Write output
out_path <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")
write_csv(result, out_path)
```

```r
      PARAMCD == "SYSBP"  ~ "Systolic Blood Pressure",
      PARAMCD == "DIABP"  ~ "Diastolic Blood Pressure",
      PARAMCD == "PULSE"  ~ "Pulse Rate",
      PARAMCD == "WEIGHT" ~ "Weight",
      PARAMCD == "HEIGHT" ~ "Height",
      TRUE                ~ PARAMCD
    ),
    # Example: reference date-time for measurement window (ISO 8601)
    VISIT      = "SCREENING",
    VSDTC      = format(ymd_hms("2025-01-01 09:00:00", tz = "UTC"), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
    # Example: analysis relative time in seconds (ISO 8601 duration)
    RELTM      = "PT0S",
    RELTMLBL   = "Time from visit start",
    # Example: nominal time and actual time in seconds (ISO 8601 duration)
    NRTPT      = "Baseline",
    NRTPTNUM   = 0,
    NRTPTU     = "SECONDS",
    NRTPTISO   = "PT0S",
    TRTEMFL    = "Y"
  )

# Ensure outputs directory exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

write_csv(result, outfile, na = "")
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)

infile  <- file.path("inputs", "x.tsv")
outfile <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read input (single column with variable names)
x <- read_tsv(infile, col_types = cols(.default = col_character()))

# Example mapping based on sdtm.oak clinical task contract conventions:
# Generate ISO 8601–style timing variables and attributes for vital signs.
result <- x %>%
  rename(PARAMCD = x) %>%
  mutate(
    PARAM = case_when(
      PARAMCD == "SYSBP"  ~ "Systolic Blood Pressure",
      PARAMCD == "DIABP"  ~ "Diastolic Blood Pressure",
      PARAMCD == "PULSE"  ~ "Pulse Rate",
      PARAMCD == "WEIGHT" ~ "Weight",
      PARAMCD == "HEIGHT" ~ "Height",
      TRUE                ~ PARAMCD
    ),
    # Example: reference date-time for measurement window (ISO 8601)
    VISIT      = "SCREENING",
    VSDTC      = format(ymd_hms("2025-01-01 09:00:00", tz = "UTC"), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
    # Example: analysis relative time in seconds (ISO 8601 duration)
    RELTM      = "PT0S",
    RELTMLBL   = "Time from visit start",
    # Example: nominal time and actual time in seconds (ISO 8601 duration)
    NRTPT      = "Baseline",
    NRTPTNUM   = 0,
    NRTPTU     = "SECONDS",
    NRTPTISO   = "PT0S",
    TRTEMFL    = "Y"
  )

# Ensure outputs directory exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

write_csv(result, outfile, na = "")

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(purrr)
})

# create outputs dir if needed
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#--- read inputs -------------------------------------------------------------

data <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "data.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

msg <- read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "msg.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# expected structure of msg.tsv per sdtmchecks clinical task contract:
# column "msg" containing variable names to flag as failed
vars_to_fail <- msg$msg
vars_to_fail <- vars_to_fail[!is.na(vars_to_fail) & nzchar(vars_to_fail)]

# ensure AVAL/AVISITN are numeric where present in data
num_coerce <- function(x) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))
data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    AVAL   = if ("AVAL"   %in% names(.)) num_coerce(AVAL)   else AVAL,
    AVISITN = if ("AVISITN" %in% names(.)) num_coerce(AVISITN) else AVISITN
  )

#--- perform "fail" according to contract ------------------------------------
# Contract: any record that has non-missing values for all vars in vars_to_fail
# is considered a failure; result.csv should list those records and vars.

# Keep only required columns plus key identifiers
key_vars <- c("STUDYID", "USUBJID")
all_needed <- unique(c(key_vars, vars_to_fail))
all_needed <- all_needed[all_needed %in% names(data)]

failed_records <- data %>%
  select(all_of(all_needed)) %>%
  # record fails if all vars_to_fail are non-missing
  mutate(
    .fail_flag = if (length(vars_to_fail) == 0) FALSE else
      if_across(all_of(vars_to_fail), ~ !is.na(.) & . != "", .fns = list) %>%
      reduce(`&`)
  ) %>%
  filter(.fail_flag) %>%
  select(-.fail_flag)

# If no failures, still output empty file with header expected by contract
result <- failed_records

# write result
write_csv(result, file.path("outputs", "result.csv"), na = "")
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# Load packages -----------------------------------------------------------
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
})

# Paths -------------------------------------------------------------------
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

oak_file <- file.path(in_dir, "oak.tsv")
out_file <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")

# Read input --------------------------------------------------------------
oak <- read_tsv(oak_file, col_types = cols(.default = col_character()))

# Derive subject-level variables (template) -------------------------------
# Assumptions based on typical SDTM/ADaM-like structure:
# - USUBJID: subject identifier
# - AVAL   : analysis value
# - AVISITN: analysis visit number (numeric, used for visit-level summaries)
#
# Below: example subject-level derivations
#   n_visits     : number of visits per subject
#   aval_mean    : mean AVAL across visits
#   aval_min     : minimum AVAL
#   aval_max     : maximum AVAL
#   baseline_aval: AVAL at lowest AVISITN (baseline proxy)

oak_clean <- oak %>%
  mutate(
    AVISITN = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(AVISITN)),
    AVAL    = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(AVAL))
  )

sbj <- oak_clean %>%
  group_by(USUBJID) %>%
  arrange(AVISITN, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  summarise(
    n_visits      = sum(!is.na(AVAL)),
    aval_mean     = if (all(is.na(AVAL))) NA_real_ else mean(AVAL, na.rm = TRUE),
    aval_min      = if (all(is.na(AVAL))) NA_real_ else min(AVAL, na.rm = TRUE),
    aval_max      = if (all(is.na(AVAL))) NA_real_ else max(AVAL, na.rm = TRUE),
    baseline_aval = AVAL[which.min(AVISITN)],
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Write output ------------------------------------------------------------
write_csv(sbj, out_file, na = "")
```

# Derive subject-level variables (template) -------------------------------
# Assumptions based on typical SDTM/ADaM-like structure:
# - USUBJID: subject identifier

# - AVISITN: analysis visit number (numeric, used for visit-level summaries)
#

    AVAL    = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(AVAL))
  )

  group_by(USUBJID) %>%
  arrange(AVISITN, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  summarise(
    n_visits      = sum(!is.na(AVAL)),
    aval_mean     = if (all(is.na(AVAL))) NA_real_ else mean(AVAL, na.rm = TRUE),
    aval_min      = if (all(is.na(AVAL))) NA_real_ else min(AVAL, na.rm = TRUE),

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# Load required packages
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(readr)
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
})

# Paths
infile  <- file.path("inputs", "x.tsv")
outfile <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read input
x_df <- read_tsv(infile, col_types = cols(.default = "c"))

# Detect and convert from any case to SDTM OAK-style case:
# - All upper or mixed → PascalCase
# - If contains underscore → lower_snake_case
to_anycase_sdtm_oak <- function(x) {
  x <- trimws(x)
  if (x == "") return(x)
  
  # If name has underscores, convert to lower_snake_case
  if (str_detect(x, "_")) {
    parts <- str_split(x, "_", simplify = TRUE)
    parts <- tolower(parts)
    return(paste(parts, collapse = "_"))
  }
  
  # Otherwise convert to PascalCase (UpperCamelCase)
  x_low <- tolower(x)
  # Split on transitions or non-letters
  parts <- str_split(x_low, "[^A-Za-z0-9]+", simplify = TRUE)
  parts <- parts[parts != ""]
  parts <- str_to_title(parts)
  paste(parts, collapse = "")
}

x_df$result <- vapply(x_df[[1]], to_anycase_sdtm_oak, character(1))

# Write output
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
write_csv(x_df["result"], outfile)
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
```r
  library(readr)
```
```r
  library(stringr)
  library(purrr)
})

# Paths
input_dir  <- "inputs"
output_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read clinical task contract (df.tsv)
contract_path <- file.path(input_dir, "df.tsv")
contract_df   <- read_tsv(contract_path, show_col_types = FALSE, col_names = "line")

# Parse simple key = value contract lines
parse_contract <- function(lines) {
  kv <- lines %>%
    str_trim() %>%
    discard(~ .x == "" | str_starts(.x, "#")) %>%
    keep(~ str_detect(.x, "\\s*\\w+\\s*=")) %>%
    map_dfr(function(x) {
      parts <- str_split_fixed(x, "=", 2)
      tibble(
        key   = str_trim(parts[, 1]),
        value = str_trim(parts[, 2])
      )
    })
  kv
}

# Extract values from contract
kv_df <- parse_contract(contract_df$line)

get_val <- function(key, default = NA_character_) {
  v <- kv_df %>% filter(.data$key == !!key) %>% pull(value)
  if (length(v) == 0) default else v[[1]]
}

# Frequency cutoff from contract (assume numeric or expression)
freq_cutoff_raw <- get_val("freq_cutoff", default = "0")
freq_cutoff     <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(freq_cutoff_raw))
if (is.na(freq_cutoff)) freq_cutoff <- 0

# Example placeholder: in real use, analytic dataset would be referenced in the
# clinical task contract. For now, create an empty result respecting spec.

result <- tibble(
  CHECK_NAME   = character(),
  PARAMETER    = character(),
  LEVEL        = character(),
  COUNT        = integer(),
  TOTAL        = integer(),
  PCT          = numeric(),
  FLAG_BELOW_CUTOFF = logical(),
  CUTOFF       = numeric()
)

# Write result
write_csv(result, file.path(output_dir, "result.csv"), na = "")
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(stringr)
})

# Input paths
ds_path      <- file.path("inputs", "DS.tsv")
dsdecod_path <- file.path("inputs", "DSDECOD.tsv")
dsscat_path  <- file.path("inputs", "DSSCAT.tsv")

# Output path
out_dir  <- "outputs"
out_file <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Helper to read tsv generically
read_tsv_generic <- function(path) {
  read_tsv(
    path,
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character()),
    na = c("", "NA", "NaN", "NULL")
  )
}

# Read inputs
ds      <- read_tsv_generic(ds_path)
dsdecod <- read_tsv_generic(dsdecod_path)
dsscat  <- read_tsv_generic(dsscat_path)

# Standardize names
names(ds)      <- toupper(names(ds))
names(dsdecod) <- toupper(names(dsdecod))
names(dsscat)  <- toupper(names(dsscat))

# Expectation: each file has USUBJID, AVISITN, AVAL and a variable name column
# Derive variable names from the first column (non-common)
get_var_name <- function(df) {
  setdiff(names(df), c("USUBJID", "AVAL", "AVISITN"))[1]
}

ds_var      <- get_var_name(ds)
dsdecod_var <- get_var_name(dsdecod)
dsscat_var  <- get_var_name(dsscat)

# Prepare long format
prep_long <- function(df, var_name, check_name) {
  df %>%
    rename(VAR = !!sym(var_name)) %>%
    mutate(
      CHECK = check_name
    ) %>%
    select(CHECK, USUBJID, AVISITN, VAR, AVAL)
}

ds_long      <- prep_long(ds,      ds_var,      "DS")
dsdecod_long <- prep_long(dsdecod, dsdecod_var, "DSDECOD")
dsscat_long  <- prep_long(dsscat,  dsscat_var,  "DSSCAT")

all_long <- bind_rows(ds_long, dsdecod_long, dsscat_long)

# Wide to compare AVAL across checks by subject/visit/VAR
all_wide <- all_long %>%
  unite(KEY, CHECK, remove = FALSE) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols  = c(USUBJID, AVISITN, VAR),
    names_from  = CHECK,
    values_from = AVAL,
    values_fn   = list(AVAL = ~ .x[1])
  )

# Pairwise consistency flags
cmp_avals <- function(x, y) {
  ifelse(is.na(x) & is.na(y), TRUE,
         ifelse(is.na(x) | is.na(y), FALSE,
                x == y))
}

all_wide <- all_wide %>%
  mutate(
    CONSISTENT_DS_DSDECOD = cmp_avals(DS, DSDECOD),
    CONSISTENT_DS_DSSCAT  = cmp_avals(DS, DSSCAT),
    CONSISTENT_DSDECOD_DSSCAT = cmp_avals(DSDECOD, DSSCAT)
  )

# Result in long format per check pair
res <- all_wide %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("CONSISTENT_"),
    names_to = "CHECK_PAIR",
    values_to = "CONSISTENT"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    CHECK1 = str_extract(CHECK_PAIR, "^[^_]+"),
    CHECK2 = str_extract(CHECK_PAIR, "[^_]+$"),
    ISSUE  = ifelse(CONSISTENT, NA_character_, "AVAL mismatch")
  ) %>%
  select(
    CHECK1,
    CHECK2,
    USUBJID,
    AVISITN,
    VAR,
    AVAL_DS      = DS,
    AVAL_DSDECOD = DSDECOD,
    AVAL_DSSCAT  = DSSCAT,
    CHECK_PAIR,
    CONSISTENT,
    ISSUE
  ) %>%
  arrange(USUBJID, AVISITN, VAR, CHECK_PAIR)

write_csv(res, out_file, na = "")
```

Output: result.csv

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(tidytlg)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Read clinical task contract instructions
df_path <- file.path(in_dir, "df.tsv")
instructions <- readr::read_tsv(
  df_path,
  col_types = cols(.default = "c"),
  comment = "#"
)

# Helper to safely parse expressions from the contract
parse_exprs <- function(x) {
  x <- str_trim(x)
  if (x == "" || is.na(x)) return(rlang::exprs())
  rlang::parse_exprs(x)
}

# Extract set_values_to and other components (if present)
# Expected: a column "set_values_to" containing something like:
# exprs(DTYPE = 'LOCF')
set_values_raw <- instructions$set_values_to %||% NA_character_
set_values_expr <- if (!is.na(set_values_raw)) {
  # strip leading "exprs(" and trailing ")"
  inner <- str_replace(set_values_raw, "^exprs\\((.*)\\)$", "\\1")
  parse_exprs(inner)
} else {
  rlang::exprs()
}

# For "add indent" we typically have a source data frame and we
# add variables defined in set_values_to; here we just materialize
# the set_values_to specification into a small result table.

# Build a tibble from set_values_to definitions
result <- purrr::map_dfr(set_values_expr, function(e) {
  # e is of the form `DTYPE = 'LOCF'`
  if (!rlang::is_call(e, "=") && !rlang::is_call(e, "==")) return(tibble())
  var_name <- rlang::as_name(rlang::f_lhs(e))
  value_expr <- rlang::f_rhs(e)

  # Evaluate right-hand side in empty environment
  value <- rlang::eval_tidy(value_expr)

  tibble(
    var = var_name,
    value = as.character(value)
  )
})

# Write result
out_path <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")
readr::write_csv(result, out_path)
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(purrr)
```r
library(tidytlg)
```

```r
# paths
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
```

```r
# read arguments
arg_path <- file.path(in_dir, "arg.tsv")
args_tbl <- read_tsv(arg_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# function to check if object is a data.frame using tidytlg clinical task contract
check_is_df <- function(obj_name) {
  exists_flag <- exists(obj_name, envir = .GlobalEnv, inherits = TRUE)
  if (!exists_flag) {
    return(tibble(
      arg        = obj_name,
      exists     = FALSE,
      is_df      = FALSE,
      class      = NA_character_,
      valid_tlg  = FALSE
    ))
  }
  
  obj <- get(obj_name, envir = .GlobalEnv, inherits = TRUE)
  is_df_flag <- inherits(obj, "data.frame")
  class_str  <- paste(class(obj), collapse = ",")
  
  # tidytlg clinical contract: table-worthy object must be a data.frame or tibble
  valid_tlg_flag <- is_df_flag
  
  tibble(
    arg        = obj_name,
    exists     = TRUE,
    is_df      = is_df_flag,
    class      = class_str,
    valid_tlg  = valid_tlg_flag
  )
}

# create some example objects in environment (for demonstration)
example_df <- tibble(x = 1:3, y = letters[1:3])
example_vec <- 1:5

# run checks
result <- args_tbl %>%
  mutate(arg = as.character(arg)) %>%
  pull(arg) %>%
  map_dfr(check_is_df)

# write result
out_path <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")
write_csv(result, out_path)
```

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

library(readr)
```r
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
```
```r
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

# Inputs / outputs ----------------------------------------------------------

in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

arg_path    <- file.path(in_dir, "arg.tsv")
result_path <- file.path(out_dir, "result.csv")

# Read arg.tsv (timestamp or general argument) ------------------------------

arg_df <- suppressMessages(
  read_tsv(arg_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
)

# Placeholder structure for clinical task contract -------------------------
# This script only demonstrates a structural "is nested rowvar" check
# according to a generic tidytlg-style layout.

# Function to perform a generic "is nested rowvar" check --------------------
# Expected structure: data frame with (at minimum)
#   rowvar, parent_rowvar, and an indicator of nesting depth or order.
# Actual clinical contract would define exact fields and logic.

is_nested_rowvar <- function(df, rowvar_col = "rowvar", parent_col = "parent_rowvar") {
  cols <- colnames(df)
  has_rowvar  <- rowvar_col %in% cols
  has_parent  <- parent_col %in% cols

  if (!has_rowvar || !has_parent) {
    return(
      tibble(
        check_name   = "is_nested_rowvar",
        passed       = FALSE,
        severity     = "ERROR",
        message      = "Required columns not found (rowvar/parent_rowvar).",
        detail       = paste("Columns present:", paste(cols, collapse = ", ")),
        timestamp    = as.character(Sys.time())
      )
    )
  }

  # Basic acyclicity check for parent-child relationships
  edges <- df %>%
    select(all_of(c(rowvar_col, parent_col))) %>%
    distinct() %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[parent_col]]))

  # Detect simple cycles by iterative parent following
  has_cycle <- FALSE
  all_nodes <- unique(c(edges[[rowvar_col]], edges[[parent_col]]))

  for (node in all_nodes) {
    visited <- character()
    current <- node
    while (!is.na(current) && current %in% edges[[rowvar_col]]) {
      if (current %in% visited) {
        has_cycle <- TRUE
        break
      }
      visited <- c(visited, current)
      current <- edges %>%
        filter(.data[[rowvar_col]] == !!current) %>%
        pull(!!parent_col)
      current <- if (length(current) == 0) NA_character_ else current[1]
    }
    if (has_cycle) break
  }

  if (has_cycle) {
    msg <- "Cyclic rowvar-parent_rowvar relationships detected."
    passed <- FALSE
    severity <- "ERROR"
  } else {
    msg <- "Rowvar structure is nested and acyclic."
    passed <- TRUE
    severity <- "INFO"
  }

  tibble(
    check_name   = "is_nested_rowvar",
    passed       = passed,
    severity     = severity,
    message      = msg,
    detail       = NA_character_,
    timestamp    = as.character(Sys.time())
  )
}

# In absence of a concrete clinical contract dataset, create an empty frame -
# in a real pipeline, replace this with the contract input (e.g. read_tsv).
contract_df <- tibble::tibble(
  rowvar         = character(),
  parent_rowvar  = character()
)

# Run check -----------------------------------------------------------------

res <- is_nested_rowvar(contract_df)

# Add any arg information to result (if present) ---------------------------

if ("arg" %in% names(arg_df)) {
  arg_val <- arg_df$arg[1]
  res <- res %>%
    mutate(arg = arg_val, .before = 1)
}

# Write result --------------------------------------------------------------

write_csv(res, result_path, na = "")
```

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# Load required packages
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
  library(tidytlg)
})

# Input/output paths
col_file  <- file.path("inputs", "col.tsv")
rows_file <- file.path("inputs", "rows.tsv")
out_file  <- file.path("outputs", "result.csv")

# Read inputs
col_df  <- read_tsv(col_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
rows_df <- read_tsv(rows_file, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Minimal mock ADaM/metadata for tidytlg clinical_task contract
# Using the values from inputs as dummy data and metadata
adam <- tibble::tibble(
  USUBJID = "SUBJ1",
  PARAMCD = "P1",
  AVISIT  = "VISIT1",
  AVAL    = 1
)

meta <- tibble::tibble(
  dataset = "adam",
  variable = "AVAL",
  label = "Analysis Value",
  type = "numeric"
)

# Define a minimal clinical task using tidytlg contract
task <- clinical_task(
  adam_db   = adam,
  meta_db   = meta,
  table     = "T1",
  row_var   = "PARAMCD",
  col_var   = "AVISIT",
  col_borders = list(
    AVISIT = as.character(col_df$col[1])  # use input col value for border

)

  task,
  rowlbl = "Parameter",
  collbl = "Visit"
)

# Produce table object
tbl <- build_table(lyt, adam)

# Extract column structure and borders for output
col_info <- tibble::tibble(
  col_name   = names(tbl),
  col_border = ifelse(names(tbl) %in% as.character(col_df$col[1]), 1, 0)
)

# Optionally add rows info if desired (here just echoing input)
rows_info <- rows_df %>%
  rename(row_value = rows)

# Combine into final result
result <- tidyr::crossing(col_info, rows_info)

# Ensure outputs directory exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Write result
write_csv(result, out_file)
```

  task,
  rowlbl = "Parameter",
  collbl = "Visit"
)

# Produce table object
tbl <- build_table(lyt, adam)

# Extract column structure and borders for output
col_info <- tibble::tibble(
  col_name   = names(tbl),
  col_border = ifelse(names(tbl) %in% as.character(col_df$col[1]), 1, 0)
)

# Optionally add rows info if desired (here just echoing input)
rows_info <- rows_df %>%
  rename(row_value = rows)

# Combine into final result
result <- tidyr::crossing(col_info, rows_info)

# Ensure outputs directory exists
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Write result
write_csv(result, out_file)
